summary: OVN-K multicast silently disabled: enable-multicast in wrong config section causes gcfg to discard it
description:
## Description of problem

The OVN-Kubernetes multicast feature is silently disabled at runtime even when the cluster administrator sets \`enable-multicast=true\` in the OVN-K config file. The root cause is a gcfg parser misconfiguration: \`enable-multicast\` is written under the \`\[ovnkubernetesfeature\]\` section in \`ovnkube.conf\`, but \`EnableMulticast\` is a **global** config variable in \`config.go\` — not a field inside the \`OVNKubernetesFeature\` struct. The gcfg parser emits a warning and silently discards the value, leaving \`config.EnableMulticast = false\` at runtime.

As a result, no multicast ACLs are created in the OVN NB database, and all cross-node multicast traffic is dropped (100% packet loss).

**Warning in OVN-K logs:**

```java
W config.go:2724: can't store data at section "ovnkubernetesfeature", variable "enable-multicast"
```

**OVN NB DB symptom (broken cluster):**

* \`clusterRtrPortGroup acls: \[\]\` (empty — no multicast ACLs)
* No \`mcast_snoop\` on node logical switches
* Zero per-namespace multicast ACLs created

## Version-Release number of selected component

4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-03-17-033403 (GCP nightly, FAILED)

4.22.0-ec.2 (CNO delivers \`--enable-multicast\` as CLI flag instead of config file entry) PASSED

## How reproducible

Always

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Deploy an OCP cluster with OVN-Kubernetes as the network plugin and multicast enabled
2. Create a namespace with multicast annotation:

    ```java
    oc annotate namespace <ns> k8s.ovn.org/multicast-enabled=true
    ```
3. Deploy 3 pods: 2 on node0, 1 on node1 (cross-node)
4. Install \`omping\` and run multicast test:

    ```java
    omping -c 5 <pod0-ip> <pod1-ip> <pod2-ip>
    ```
5. Check OVN NB DB for multicast ACLs:

    ```java
    oc exec -n openshift-ovn-kubernetes <ovnkube-pod> -- ovn-nbctl find ACL | grep -i mcast
    ```

## Actual results

* \`omping\` reports \`multicast, xmt/rcv/%loss = 5/0/100%\` (cross-node, 100% loss)
* OVN NB DB has empty ACL list for clusterRtrPortGroup
* OVN-K logs contain: \`W config.go:2724: can't store data at section "ovnkubernetesfeature", variable "enable-multicast"\`
* \`config.EnableMulticast = false\` at runtime despite config file setting

## Expected results

* Multicast traffic flows between pods across nodes
* OVN NB creates 2 AllowInterNode ACLs on clusterRtrPortGroup and 2 DefaultDeny ACLs on clusterPortGroup
* \`mcast_snoop=true\` and \`mcast_querier=true\` on all node logical switches
* Per-namespace ACLs created when \`multicast-enabled\` annotation is applied

## Additional info

**Root cause:** \`enable-multicast\` is placed under \`\[ovnkubernetesfeature\]\` in \`ovnkube.conf\` by CNO, but it maps to \`OvnKubeConfig.EnableMulticast\` (global scope) in \`config.go\`. The gcfg library ignores section-scoped values for global variables.

**confirmed in 4.22.0-ec.2:** CNO now passes \`--enable-multicast\` as a CLI flag to \`ovnkube-controller\`, bypassing the config file section issue entirel

Please fill in the following template while reporting a bug and provide as much relevant information as possible.    
Doing so will give us the best chance to find a prompt resolution.

---

### Affected Platforms

Is it an:

* \[x\] internal CI failure  
* \[ \] customer issue / SD  
* \[x\] internal Red Hat testing failure  

---

### If it is an internal Red Hat testing failure

* Please share a kubeconfig or creds to a live cluster for the assignee to debug/troubleshoot along with reproducer steps    
  (especially if it's a telco use case like ICNI, secondary bridges or BM + kubevirt).

---

### If it is a CI failure

* Did it happen in different CI lanes? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* Did it happen in both SDN and OVN jobs? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* Did it happen on other platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP, Bare Metal)? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* When did the failure start happening? Please provide the **UTC timestamp** of the networking outage window from a sample failure run.  
* If it's a connectivity issue, please provide:

    * srcNode, srcIP, srcNamespace, and srcPodName  
    * dstNode, dstIP, dstNamespace, and dstPodName  
    * traffic path (examples: pod→pod, pod→external, pod→svc, pod→node, etc.)
    

---

### If it is a Customer / SD issue

* Provide enough information in the bug description that Engineering doesn’t need to read the entire case history.  
* Don’t presume that Engineering has access to Sales

summary: IBM VPC SDK v0.80.0 bump breaks build due to removed NetworkACLRuleProtocolAllConst constant
description:
## Description of problem

Dependabot PR [#7717](https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/pull/7717) bumps the `ibm-github-dependencies` group with 6 updates, including `github.com/IBM/vpc-go-sdk` from v0.68.0 to v0.80.0. The new version of the IBM VPC SDK removed the constant `vpcv1.NetworkACLRuleProtocolAllConst`, which is still referenced by the vendored `cluster-api-provider-ibmcloud` at `vendor/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-ibmcloud/api/v1beta2/types.go:323`. This causes a compilation failure.

The full list of dependency bumps in PR #7717:

| Package | From | To |
| --- | --- | --- |
| github.com/IBM-Cloud/power-go-client | 1.11.0 | 1.14.4 |
| github.com/IBM/go-sdk-core/v5 | 5.19.1 | 5.21.2 |
| github.com/IBM/ibm-cos-sdk-go | 1.12.2 | 1.13.0 |
| github.com/IBM/networking-go-sdk | 0.51.4 | 0.51.16 |
| github.com/IBM/platform-services-go-sdk | 0.81.0 | 0.94.2 |
| github.com/IBM/vpc-go-sdk | 0.68.0 | 0.80.0 |

## Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable)

main branch (openshift/hypershift)

## How reproducible

Always

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Cherry-pick dependabot PR #7717 onto the hypershift main branch
2. Run `go build ./...` or `make test`

## Actual results

Build fails with:

```
vendor/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-ibmcloud/api/v1beta2/types.go:323: undefined: vpcv1.NetworkACLRuleProtocolAllConst
```

## Expected results

Build should succeed after updating IBM dependencies.

## Additional info

* Discovered during the `periodic-ci-openshift-hypershift-main-dependabot-triage` CI job run on 2026-03-13: [job artifacts](https://gcsweb-ci.apps.ci.l2s4.p1.openshiftapps.com/gcs/test-platform-results/logs/periodic-ci-openshift-hypershift-main-dependabot-triage/2032426599881641984/)
* The CI job also timed out (2h limit) while processing this failure
* The other two dependabot PRs in that run (#7949 golangci-lint and #7897 google.golang.org/api) consolidated successfully
* Resolution likely requires either: (a) updating the vendored `cluster-api-provider-ibmcloud` to a version compatible with vpc-go-sdk v0.80.0, or (b) pinning vpc-go-sdk to a version that still exports `NetworkACLRuleProtocolAllConst`

summary: synce4lConf requires explicit clock_id
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
  SyncE fails to initialize when relying on automatic clock_id detection.                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  synce4l reports clock_id is 0 and falls back to legacy mode, which then fails.       
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.20
```

**How reproducible:**

```
100% — affects any SyncE configuration that does not have an explicit
  clock_id value in synce4lConf.
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
  1. Create a PtpConfig CR with a synce4lConf section, without specifying
     an explicit clock_id under [<synce1>].
  2. Apply the PtpConfig and check the linuxptp-daemon logs.
```

**Actual results:**

```
  synce4l logs the following and exits:
    config item synce1.clock_id is 0 (0x0)
    synce_dev_ctrl_init: using legacy mode
    failure: eec_get_state_cmd is NULL on synce1
    no SyncE devices initialized

  The rendered config file (/var/run/synce4l.X.config) has no clock_id
  line in the [<synce1>] section.
```

**Expected results:**

```
  clock_id should be automatically populated from the NIC's PCI Device
  Serial Number. synce4l should initialize using the DPLL netlink interface
  and report EEC state transitions.
```

**Additional info:**

```
  Workaround: manually add clock_id to the synce4lConf string under [<synce1>].
  
```

summary: Failed to upgrade operator due to validation of CRDs when having multiple CRD versions
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
When upgrading our operator Strimzi from 0.49.1 - with CR having apiVersion set to v1beta2 - to 0.51.0 that has two supported CRD versions (v1beta2 and v1), the operator fails to upgrade due to missing required fields validation pointing to v1:

error validating existing CRs against new CRD's schema for "kafkaconnects.kafka.strimzi.io": error validating kafka.strimzi.io/v1, Kind=KafkaConnect "strimzi-test/my-connect-cluster": updated validation is too restrictive: [[].spec.groupId: Required value, [].spec.configStorageTopic: Required value, [].spec.statusStorageTopic: Required value, [].spec.offsetStorageTopic: Required value]

This error is shown because in v1beta2 the fields are missing, but in v1 they are present and required.
We have Strimzi 0.49.x, 0.50.x, and 0.51.x versions used as "bridge" versions for migrating from one CRD version to the other, all these versions contain both of the CRD versions to make possible for users to migrate to v1 any time they want.
Both versions (v1beta2 and v1) are present in all CRDs, v1beta2 is configured with "storage: true, served: true", v1 is configured with "storage: false, served: true".
Users and customers have possibility to migrate to v1 any time using our conversion tool.
Using YAML manifests and Helm the upgrade works fine, it's just OLM that fails because of the validation.

From our point of view the validation should be done against the apiVersion the resource is using - so if it is using v1beta2, it should use v1beta2 for validation.
This breaks any upgrade between these versions.
Removing CRDs and CRs is not acceptable, because it would mean major disruption for our customers' production environment.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
OLM v0, OCP 4.21 (but I assume it affects other as well)
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Deploy Strimzi 0.49.x from the 0.49.x channel
2. Deploy Kafka (https://github.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/blob/0.48.0/examples/kafka/kafka-ephemeral.yaml) and KafkaConnect (https://github.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/blob/0.48.0/examples/connect/kafka-connect.yaml) - the 0.48.0 is last version which contains the v1beta2 examples, that's why I'm adding those links here
3. Upgrade to Strimzi 0.50.x or 0.51.x by changing the channel
4. Wait for installation Pending and error status in the subscription of the operator
```

**Actual results:**

```
Validation error like this in Subscription section of the operator:

error validating existing CRs against new CRD's schema for "kafkaconnects.kafka.strimzi.io": error validating kafka.strimzi.io/v1, Kind=KafkaConnect "strimzi-test/my-connect-cluster": updated validation is too restrictive: [[].spec.groupId: Required value, [].spec.configStorageTopic: Required value, [].spec.statusStorageTopic: Required value, [].spec.offsetStorageTopic: Required value]
```

**Expected results:**

```
Upgrade will happen a

summary: Secondary-scheduler: do not set resource limits by default
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
According to scalability requirements the secondary-scheduler (topology-aware-scheduler) resource requests 
should not have limits set, in order to support scaleability requirements.

We already enabled this option on 4.20:
https://github.com/openshift-kni/numaresources-operator/pull/1689

The feature was not set as the default, despite the requirement for it to be the standard option from version 4.22 onwards.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.22
```

**How reproducible:**

```

summary: Feature gate lists can be set when FeatureSet is empty
description:
## Root cause

Commit `02b1948a8` intentionally removed the validation check.

Before that commit, `validateFeatureGates()` at `pkg/config/apiserver.go:196` had:

```go
case "":
    if len(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled) > 0 || len(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Disabled) > 0 {
        return fmt.Errorf("CustomNoUpgrade enabled/disabled lists must be empty when FeatureSet is empty")
    }
    return nil
```

Now it's simply:

```go
case "":
    return nil
```

When `featureSet` is `""` (empty string), validation returns `nil` immediately — **regardless** of what's in the `customNoUpgrade.enabled/disabled` lists.

This means a user can write:

```yaml
featureGates:
  featureSet: ""
  customNoUpgrade:
    enabled: ["SomeFeature"]
```

and MicroShift will:

* Pass validation (no checks run)
* Apply `SomeFeature=true` to kube-apiserver (via `ToApiserverArgs`)
* **Not** create an upgrade lock file
* Allow upgrades that could break the cluster due to the custom feature gate

The old validation was protecting against this exact scenario. The commit message calls it "overly strict," but it was preventing a configuration where feature gates get applied without the upgrade safety net.  

## Full execution path for `featureSet: ""` + `customNoUpgrade.enabled: ["SomeFeature"]`

### 1. Config merging (`config.go:429-434`)

```go
if u.ApiServer.FeatureGates.FeatureSet != "" {  // FALSE — "" == ""
    c.ApiServer.FeatureGates.FeatureSet = ...   // SKIPPED
}
if len(u.ApiServer.FeatureGates.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled) > 0 {  // TRUE
    c.ApiServer.FeatureGates.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled = ...      // COPIED
}
```

**Result:** `FeatureSet=""`, `CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled=["SomeFeature"]`

### 2. Validation (`apiserver.go:190-197`)

```go
if fg == nil || reflect.DeepEqual(*fg, FeatureGates{}) {  // FALSE — Enabled list is non-nil
    return nil
}
switch fg.FeatureSet {
case "":
    return nil  // ← EXITS HERE. No further checks.
```

**Result:** validation passes.

### 3. Upgrade lock (`featuregate_lock.go:57`)

```go
if fgCfg.FeatureSet != "" {  // FALSE
    return createFeatureGateLockFile(fgCfg)  // SKIPPED
}
```

**Result:** no lock file created. Upgrades not blocked.

### 4. Kube-apiserver args (`kube-apiserver.go:172`, `apiserver.go:163-176`)

```go
featureGateArgs, err := cfg.ApiServer.FeatureGates.ToApiserverArgs()
// ToApiserverArgs does NOT check FeatureSet. It unconditionally processes:
addFeatures(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled, true)  // → "SomeFeature=true"
```

Then at line 235:

```go
"feature-gates": featureGateArgs,  // → ["SomeFeature=true", "UserNamespacesSupport=true", ...]
```

**Result:** `SomeFeature=true` is passed to kube-apiserver.

summary: HyperShift filtering of CVO manifests should respect feature-set annotation
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
A combination of the HyperShift manifest inclusion logic for hosted CVO manifests and recent 
changes for CRD filenames in the openshift/api repository disallows updating the 
openshift/api module in the openshift/cluster-version-operator repository.

HyperShift filters CVO manifests based on a filenaming pattern [1]. The pattern no longer 
applies due to recent openshift/api changes [2], and the inclusion logic of manifests is 
expected to respect the release.openshift.io/feature-set annotation for feature set inclusion.

[1]: https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/blob/23fc7eb379fa973ddd1fefd6cc9b4613ec4dc976/control-plane-operator/controllers/hostedcontrolplane/v2/cvo/deployment.go#L202-L241
[2]: https://github.com/openshift/api/pull/2637
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.22/main
```

**How reproducible:**

```
1/1
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Try to bump the openshift/api module in the CVO repository
2. Observe failing hypershift-conformance CI job run
```

**Actual results:**

```
The CVO repository [3] is now unable to bump the openshift/api dependency, as this breaks 
HyperShift presubmit CI jobs. The HyperShift ends up applying CRDs and CRs that it wasn’t supposed to.

A first potential solution is to teach the relevant HyperShift filtering to respect 
the release.openshift.io/feature-set annotation during the inclusion.

[3]: https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator
```

**Expected results:**

```
HyperShift respects the annotation. The CVO repository is able to bump the dependency.
```

**Additional info:**

```
Joel Speed has started working on a fix [4]. This ticket acts to transparently represent the issue and coordinate efforts if needed.

[4]: https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/pull/7984
```

summary: Admin Network Policy does not remove nodes from .status.conditions (Ready-In-Zone-<node-name>) consuming all the CPU on masters
description:
**Description of problem:**

The ANP .status.conditions contains Ready-In-Zone-<node-name> entries for every node that has ever existed since the ANP was created. Currently there are \~160+ zone condition entries, but only 55 nodes are active. The 105 extra entries correspond to nodes that were deleted (spot instance evictions, autoscaler scale-downs, etc.). These stale entries are never cleaned up.

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

4.18

**How reproducible:**

Always

Create a simple Admin Network Policy to allow ingress for all namespaces

**Steps to Reproduce:**  
1\. Install Openshift 4.18  
2\. Install an Admin Network Policy as following

apiVersion: [policy.networking.k8s.io/v1alpha1](http://policy.networking.k8s.io/v1alpha1)  
kind: AdminNetworkPolicy  
metadata:  
  name: allow-intra-egress  
spec:  
  priority: 1  
  subject:  
    namespaces: {}  
  egress:  
    - name: allow-intra-egress  
      action: Allow  
      to:  
        - networks:  
            - 10.225.0.0/16

  
3\. Delete nodes  
4\. Deleted node is still in .status.conditions and is never removed  
5\. Observe CPU consumption on Masters. It increases over time when additional nodes are removed. On cloud providers using spot or ephemeral instances reclaimed all the time it exhausts all the available CPU. In addition Admin Network Policy object growths consuming more and more space in ETCD

**Actual results:**

.status.conditions never cleaned up consuming CPU on masters and storage in ETCD

**Expected results:**

.status.conditions properly maintains to reflect existing nodes

**Additional info:**

A similar issue as been fixed with egressFirewalls  
<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-62262</custom>

summary: [4.16] Cluster Autoscaler CAPI provider accumulates replica decrements on paused MachineDeployments causing catastrophic scale-down on unpause
description:
Description of problem:

* When a MachineDeployment's reconciliation is paused (via cluster.x-k8s.io/paused annotation or spec.pausedUntil on HostedCluster), the Cluster Autoscaler continues to identify unneeded nodes and call DeleteNodes(). Each call decrements .spec.replicas on the MachineDeployment via SetSize(replicas - 1). Since the CAPI controller is not reconciling, machines are never actually deleted — the nodes remain Ready with no deletionTimestamp. CAS sees them as unneeded again on the next loop and decrements replicas further. After enough iterations, .spec.replicas reaches minSize (1). When reconciliation resumes (unpause), the CAPI MachineDeployment controller sees .spec.replicas=1 and mass-deletes all machines down to 1, causing a catastrophic scale-down.

* This is the same class of bug as OCPBUGS-60790 but with a different trigger. The fix in OCPBUGS-60790 (upstream PR [https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8583](https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8583)) only covers the MachineDeployment rolling upgrade scenario by checking IsMachineDeploymentAndRollingOut(). It does NOT detect paused MachineDeployments.

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
4.18.27 (confirmed), likely affects all versions with CAPI provider
```

How reproducible:

```java
Reliably reproducible when MachineDeployment reconciliation is paused for extended periods while CAS is running
```

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a ROSA HCP cluster with autoscaling NodePools (minSize=1, maxSize=N), current replicas \~46
2. Trigger a pause on the HostedCluster (e.g., OADP backup sets spec.pausedUntil: true, which propagates cluster.x-k8s.io/paused annotation to MachineDeployments)
3. Wait for CAS to identify unneeded nodes (idle/underutilized)
4. CAS calls DeleteNodes() which annotates machines with cluster.x-k8s.io/delete-machine and patches .spec.replicas downward
5. Since CAPI is paused, machines are NOT deleted — nodes remain alive
6. CAS keeps decrementing .spec.replicas on subsequent loops since nodes are still present and unneeded
7. After sufficient time (\~2 hours), .spec.replicas reaches minSize (1)
8. Unpause the HostedCluster — CAPI controller resumes and scales all MachineSets to 1

Actual results:

* All three MachineSets scaled to 1 simultaneously within seconds of unpause:

    ```java
    I0308 07:06:22 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-0: 46 -> 1"
    I0308 07:06:23 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-1: 44 -> 1"
    I0308 07:06:24 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-2: 42 -> 1"
    ```

* 130+ machines entered Deleting state simultaneously
* Customer production outage (#ITN-2026-00072)
* Cluster self-recovered over \~1 hour as CAS scaled back up from 1

Expected results:

```java
CAS should not accumulate replica decrements when previous deletion requests have not been fulfilled.
```

or

```java
CAS should detect that a MachineDeployment is paused and skip scale-down actions for nodes belonging to paused MachineDeployments

summary: CLONE - kubelet terminates kube-apiserver gracefully failing consistently on ROSA
description:
Component Readiness has found multiple regressions for following test in a number of jobs and presubmits. 4.21 appears unaffected, this looks like a net new regression, but appearing only in a few targeted jobs and we're unclear why.

```java
[sig-api-machinery][Feature:APIServer][Late] kubelet terminates kube-apiserver gracefully extended [Suite:openshift/conformance/parallel]
```

summary: [4.19] Cluster Autoscaler CAPI provider accumulates replica decrements on paused MachineDeployments causing catastrophic scale-down on unpause
description:
Description of problem:

* When a MachineDeployment's reconciliation is paused (via cluster.x-k8s.io/paused annotation or spec.pausedUntil on HostedCluster), the Cluster Autoscaler continues to identify unneeded nodes and call DeleteNodes(). Each call decrements .spec.replicas on the MachineDeployment via SetSize(replicas - 1). Since the CAPI controller is not reconciling, machines are never actually deleted — the nodes remain Ready with no deletionTimestamp. CAS sees them as unneeded again on the next loop and decrements replicas further. After enough iterations, .spec.replicas reaches minSize (1). When reconciliation resumes (unpause), the CAPI MachineDeployment controller sees .spec.replicas=1 and mass-deletes all machines down to 1, causing a catastrophic scale-down.

* This is the same class of bug as OCPBUGS-60790 but with a different trigger. The fix in OCPBUGS-60790 (upstream PR [https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8583](https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8583)) only covers the MachineDeployment rolling upgrade scenario by checking IsMachineDeploymentAndRollingOut(). It does NOT detect paused MachineDeployments.

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
4.18.27 (confirmed), likely affects all versions with CAPI provider
```

How reproducible:

```java
Reliably reproducible when MachineDeployment reconciliation is paused for extended periods while CAS is running
```

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a ROSA HCP cluster with autoscaling NodePools (minSize=1, maxSize=N), current replicas \~46
2. Trigger a pause on the HostedCluster (e.g., OADP backup sets spec.pausedUntil: true, which propagates cluster.x-k8s.io/paused annotation to MachineDeployments)
3. Wait for CAS to identify unneeded nodes (idle/underutilized)
4. CAS calls DeleteNodes() which annotates machines with cluster.x-k8s.io/delete-machine and patches .spec.replicas downward
5. Since CAPI is paused, machines are NOT deleted — nodes remain alive
6. CAS keeps decrementing .spec.replicas on subsequent loops since nodes are still present and unneeded
7. After sufficient time (\~2 hours), .spec.replicas reaches minSize (1)
8. Unpause the HostedCluster — CAPI controller resumes and scales all MachineSets to 1

Actual results:

* All three MachineSets scaled to 1 simultaneously within seconds of unpause:

    ```java
    I0308 07:06:22 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-0: 46 -> 1"
    I0308 07:06:23 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-1: 44 -> 1"
    I0308 07:06:24 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-2: 42 -> 1"
    ```

* 130+ machines entered Deleting state simultaneously
* Customer production outage (#ITN-2026-00072)
* Cluster self-recovered over \~1 hour as CAS scaled back up from 1

Expected results:

```java
CAS should not accumulate replica decrements when previous deletion requests have not been fulfilled.
```

or

```java
CAS should detect that a MachineDeployment is paused and skip scale-down actions for nodes belonging to paused MachineDeployments

summary: Cloud Controller Manager pods crash with `Failed to create an OpenStack LoadBalancer client` while installing a OpenStack IPI cluster
description:
Description of problem:

```java
Installing a 4.20 cluster on OpenStack platform via IPI method fails while bootstrapping the cluster. The cloud controller manager pods crash with following error. The CCM pods constantly restart and crash with the following error. This results into credential sync failing.

~~~
F1107 11:03:17.797998       1 openstack.go:355] Failed to create an OpenStack LoadBalancer client: failed to find load-balancer v2 public endpoint for region : No suitable endpoint could be found in the service catalog.
~~~

The master nodes do get created and join the cluster but are stuck in NotReady state. 

The installation works seamlessly with same clouds.yaml file and install config file for a 4.19 cluster, but fails for 4.20 installation. As a workaround the loadbalencer is manually disabled. Appending the fields below gets the cluster installed. 

~~~
[LoadBalancer] 
enabled: false
~~~
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
‌
```

How reproducible:

```java
Install an OpenStack IPI cluster using IPI method without any details of loadbalencer in cloud.yaml file
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```java
### Configure the clouds.yaml file with any loadbalencer details
### Install a OpenStack IPI installation
### Installation fails with CCM pods crashing
```

Actual results:

```java
OpenStack IPI fails if loadbalencer details are not provided in cloud.yaml file
```

Expected results:

```java
The installation should succeed with no loadbalencer details provided in clouds.yaml file.
```

Additional info:

```java
Must gather of the failed attempt can be found here - https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1kfx-ndiKHlj18tc8tD4XQ2YTGlf9CrOq?usp=sharing
```

‌

_This is a backport clone of OCPBUGS-64842 for 4.21.z_

summary: [openshift-4.12] hermetic build failure - node-feature-discovery
description:
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-77764#icft=OCPBUGS-77764</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-77763#icft=OCPBUGS-77763</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue OCPBUGS-77651. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
Description of problem:

```java
Node Feature Discovery build fails in hermetic mode due to non-deterministic binary path handling in Dockerfile. The build uses "go install" command which places binaries in unpredictable GOPATH locations, and the Dockerfile copies from multiple inconsistent paths causing binary path conflicts.
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
OpenShift Container Platform 4.19+
  node-feature-discovery component across all affected versions
```

How reproducible:

```java
Always - when building node-feature-discovery in hermetic mode
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```java
1. Build node-feature-discovery container in hermetic mode
  2. Dockerfile copies binaries from multiple paths: /go/bin/* and /go/node-feature-discovery/bin/*
  3. "go install" command places binaries in non-deterministic GOPATH locations during hermetic build process
```

Actual results:

```java
Build fails because binaries are placed in unpredictable locations by "go install" and Dockerfile attempts to copy from multiple conflicting paths

  Error: Inconsistent binary placement causing copy failures during Docker image build process when switching from "go install" to "go build" in
  hermetic mode
```

Expected results:

```java
Build succeeds using deterministic binary paths. Should use "go build" with explicit output paths instead of "go install" and copy binaries from single consistent location in Dockerfile.
```

Additional info:

```java
Root cause: "go install" command places binaries in GOPATH-dependent locations that are not deterministic in hermetic builds, unlike "go build"
   which allows explicit output path control.

  Fix implemented: Remove redundant /go/bin/* copy command from Dockerfile and keep only the specific /go/node-feature-discovery/bin/* path. This
   ensures consistent binary placement and eliminates path conflicts during hermetic builds.

  Reference upstream work: https://github.com/openshift/node-feature-discovery/pull/163/changes
  Related hermetic conversion tracking: ART-14586
```

summary: Request are getting failed intermittently on GCP cloud where endpointpublishingstrategy is loadbalnacer.
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
Cu has cluster on GCP IPI where they want to configure whitelist IP on a route. Cu added annotation "haproxy.router.openshift.io/ip_whitelist" to whitelist an IP. However on access logs, intermittently we are seeing some of the request with client IP "100.64.x.x/10"  which is used by internal OVN. Due to which Cu is getting <NOSRV> on access logs and getting "empty reply from server" error. However when we see true client IP on access logs, the request is successfull.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.18.11
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Not reproducible in test environment
```

**Actual results:**

```
Intermittently Seeing client IP from "100.64.0.x" range on router access logs.
```

**Expected results:**

```
We should not see ovn internal IP as client IP on router access logs.
```

- [ ]

Additional info:

```java
Root cause: "go install" command places binaries in GOPATH-dependent locations that are not deterministic in hermetic builds, unlike "go build"
   which allows explicit output path control.

  Fix implemented: Remove redundant /go/bin/* copy command from Dockerfile and keep only the specific /go/node-feature-discovery/bin/* path. This
   ensures consistent binary placement and eliminates path conflicts during hermetic builds.

  Reference upstream work: https://github.com/openshift/node-feature-discovery/pull/163/changes
  Related hermetic conversion tracking: ART-14586
```

summary: [4.21] Cluster Autoscaler CAPI provider accumulates replica decrements on paused MachineDeployments causing catastrophic scale-down on unpause
description:
Description of problem:

* When a MachineDeployment's reconciliation is paused (via cluster.x-k8s.io/paused annotation or spec.pausedUntil on HostedCluster), the Cluster Autoscaler continues to identify unneeded nodes and call DeleteNodes(). Each call decrements .spec.replicas on the MachineDeployment via SetSize(replicas - 1). Since the CAPI controller is not reconciling, machines are never actually deleted — the nodes remain Ready with no deletionTimestamp. CAS sees them as unneeded again on the next loop and decrements replicas further. After enough iterations, .spec.replicas reaches minSize (1). When reconciliation resumes (unpause), the CAPI MachineDeployment controller sees .spec.replicas=1 and mass-deletes all machines down to 1, causing a catastrophic scale-down.

* This is the same class of bug as OCPBUGS-60790 but with a different trigger. The fix in OCPBUGS-60790 (upstream PR [https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8583](https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8583)) only covers the MachineDeployment rolling upgrade scenario by checking IsMachineDeploymentAndRollingOut(). It does NOT detect paused MachineDeployments.

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
4.18.27 (confirmed), likely affects all versions with CAPI provider
```

How reproducible:

```java
Reliably reproducible when MachineDeployment reconciliation is paused for extended periods while CAS is running
```

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a ROSA HCP cluster with autoscaling NodePools (minSize=1, maxSize=N), current replicas \~46
2. Trigger a pause on the HostedCluster (e.g., OADP backup sets spec.pausedUntil: true, which propagates cluster.x-k8s.io/paused annotation to MachineDeployments)
3. Wait for CAS to identify unneeded nodes (idle/underutilized)
4. CAS calls DeleteNodes() which annotates machines with cluster.x-k8s.io/delete-machine and patches .spec.replicas downward
5. Since CAPI is paused, machines are NOT deleted — nodes remain alive
6. CAS keeps decrementing .spec.replicas on subsequent loops since nodes are still present and unneeded
7. After sufficient time (\~2 hours), .spec.replicas reaches minSize (1)
8. Unpause the HostedCluster — CAPI controller resumes and scales all MachineSets to 1

Actual results:

* All three MachineSets scaled to 1 simultaneously within seconds of unpause:

    ```java
    I0308 07:06:22 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-0: 46 -> 1"
    I0308 07:06:23 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-1: 44 -> 1"
    I0308 07:06:24 - "Scaled MachineSet c512xl-2: 42 -> 1"
    ```

* 130+ machines entered Deleting state simultaneously
* Customer production outage (#ITN-2026-00072)
* Cluster self-recovered over \~1 hour as CAS scaled back up from 1

Expected results:

```java
CAS should not accumulate replica decrements when previous deletion requests have not been fulfilled.
```

or

```java
CAS should detect that a MachineDeployment is paused and skip scale-down actions for nodes belonging to paused MachineDeployments

summary: Replace personal fork with organization fork for filepath-securejoin in origin repo
description:
PR <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/openshift/origin/pull/30746</custom>  bumped library-go, which transitively upgraded <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">http://github.com/cyphar/filepath-securejoin</custom>  from v0.3.4 to v0.6.0. This upgrade introduced a breaking  
  API change that caused build failures.

  The breaking change: filepath-securejoin v0.6.0 removed MkdirAllHandle() from the root package (moved to pathrs-lite subpackage).

  The problem: opencontainers/runc (vendored transitively via Kubernetes kubelet) still calls the old securejoin.MkdirAllHandle() API, causing:  
  vendor/github.com/opencontainers/runc/libcontainer/utils/utils_unix.go:334:20: undefined: securejoin.MkdirAllHandle

  The workaround: PR #30746 introduced a personal fork with a compatibility shim:  
  <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">http://github.com/cyphar/filepath-securejoin</custom>  => <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-3">http://github.com/wangke19/filepath-securejoin-1</custom>  v0.6.2-0.20260309113322-d8b3e820b3d6

  Security concern: Personal forks create supply chain risks and should be replaced with organization forks for better transparency and auditability.

  Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable)

  4.18

  How reproducible

  Always

  Steps to Reproduce

1. Review PR <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-4">https://github.com/openshift/origin/pull/30746</custom> 
2. Check go.mod for the personal fork replace directive
3. Note the dependency chain: library-go → filepath-securejoin v0.6.0 → breaks runc

  Actual results

  The PR uses a personal fork (<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-5">http://github.com/wangke19/filepath-securejoin-1</custom> ) to work around the incompatibility.

  Expected results

  Should use an OpenShift organization fork ([http://github.com/openshift/filepath-securejoin](http://github.com/openshift/filepath-securejoin) ) for better supply chain security and team ownership.

  Additional info

* GitHub issue tracking migration: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-6">https://github.com/openshift/origin/issues/30890</custom> 
* PR with personal fork: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-7">https://github.com/openshift/origin/pull/30746</custom> 
* Potential obsolescence: PR #30866 (K8s 1.35 rebase) might remove runc entirely, making this fork unnecessary
* Migration requires OpenShift GitHub organization admin permissions

summary: MCO controller pod fails to acquire lease - disruptive tests blocked
description:
## Summary

The MCO controller pod is failing to acquire its leader election lease on arm64/aws/techpreview clusters, causing all disruptive MCO tests to fail. The controller times out after 360 seconds waiting for the lease, blocking all test execution.

## Affected Tests

The following tests are affected (all fail with the same root cause):

```java
[sig-mco][Suite:openshift/machine-config-operator/disruptive][Serial][Disruptive] MCO ocb [PolarionID:77782][OTP] A MachineOSConfig with an unfinished build can be re-build
```

```java
[sig-mco][Suite:openshift/machine-config-operator/disruptive][Serial][Disruptive] MCO ocb [PolarionID:83138][OTP] A MachineOSConfig fails to apply or degrades if invalid inputs are given
```

```java
[sig-mco][Suite:openshift/machine-config-operator/disruptive][Serial][Disruptive] MCO ocb [PolarionID:83140][OTP] A MachineOSConfig with custom containerfile definition can be successfully applied
```

```java
[sig-mco][Suite:openshift/machine-config-operator/disruptive][Serial][Disruptive] MCO ocb [PolarionID:83141][OTP] A valid MachineOSConfig leads to a successful MachineOSBuild and cleanup of its associated resources
```

Additionally, 7 other MCO disruptive tests fail with the same error in every affected run (password tests, MachineConfigNode tests, ControlPlaneMachineSet tests, osImageStream tests) - 11 tests total per run.

## Test IDs

* openshift-tests:eb8b0547cfbd47f6118f62ea5640e876
* openshift-tests:b8a4b608a7ebc82ddfeeba841a35f0f4
* openshift-tests:845193ea27c8f5c14214ea2a7c213fb6
* openshift-tests:5c28f7a26f140fbc2d44b97ea5cee498

## Component Readiness Regressions

* Regression 36412
* Regression 36417
* Regression 36420
* Regression 36428

## Error Message

All affected tests fail with:

```java
fail [github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/test/extended-priv/util.go:225]: Timed out after 360.010s.
The controller pod did not acquire the lease properly. Cannot execute any test case if the controller is not active
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
```

## Failure Pattern

* **Regression opened**: 2026-03-17
* **Regression started**: 2026-03-14 (3 consecutive failures after 9 consecutive successes)
* **Affected variants**: arm64, techpreview, ipi, ovn, aws, ha, no upgrade
* **Pattern**: Recent Regression - pass sequence FFFSSS (3 most recent runs failed, 3 prior passed)
* **Consistency**: 100% - all 3 failures show identical error
* **Global pass rate**: 78.6% across all variants (down from 100%)

## Analysis

The MCO controller pod fails to acquire its leader election lease within 360 seconds on arm64/aws/techpreview. Since the controller never becomes active, all 11 disruptive MCO tests in the suite fail. The test passes 100% on amd64/aws/techpreview but fails 50% on arm64, suggesting an arm64-specific issue with controller startup timing or resource constraints.

## Sample Failed Runs

* [Mar 16](https://prow.ci.openshift.org/view/gs/test-platform-results/logs/periodic-ci-openshift-machine-confi

summary: Feature gate lists can be set when FeatureSet is empty
description:
## Root cause

Commit `02b1948a8` intentionally removed the validation check.

Before that commit, `validateFeatureGates()` at `pkg/config/apiserver.go:196` had:

```go
case "":
    if len(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled) > 0 || len(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Disabled) > 0 {
        return fmt.Errorf("CustomNoUpgrade enabled/disabled lists must be empty when FeatureSet is empty")
    }
    return nil
```

Now it's simply:

```go
case "":
    return nil
```

When `featureSet` is `""` (empty string), validation returns `nil` immediately — **regardless** of what's in the `customNoUpgrade.enabled/disabled` lists.

This means a user can write:

```yaml
featureGates:
  featureSet: ""
  customNoUpgrade:
    enabled: ["SomeFeature"]
```

and MicroShift will:

* Pass validation (no checks run)
* Apply `SomeFeature=true` to kube-apiserver (via `ToApiserverArgs`)
* **Not** create an upgrade lock file
* Allow upgrades that could break the cluster due to the custom feature gate

The old validation was protecting against this exact scenario. The commit message calls it "overly strict," but it was preventing a configuration where feature gates get applied without the upgrade safety net.  

## Full execution path for `featureSet: ""` + `customNoUpgrade.enabled: ["SomeFeature"]`

### 1. Config merging (`config.go:429-434`)

```go
if u.ApiServer.FeatureGates.FeatureSet != "" {  // FALSE — "" == ""
    c.ApiServer.FeatureGates.FeatureSet = ...   // SKIPPED
}
if len(u.ApiServer.FeatureGates.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled) > 0 {  // TRUE
    c.ApiServer.FeatureGates.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled = ...      // COPIED
}
```

**Result:** `FeatureSet=""`, `CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled=["SomeFeature"]`

### 2. Validation (`apiserver.go:190-197`)

```go
if fg == nil || reflect.DeepEqual(*fg, FeatureGates{}) {  // FALSE — Enabled list is non-nil
    return nil
}
switch fg.FeatureSet {
case "":
    return nil  // ← EXITS HERE. No further checks.
```

**Result:** validation passes.

### 3. Upgrade lock (`featuregate_lock.go:57`)

```go
if fgCfg.FeatureSet != "" {  // FALSE
    return createFeatureGateLockFile(fgCfg)  // SKIPPED
}
```

**Result:** no lock file created. Upgrades not blocked.

### 4. Kube-apiserver args (`kube-apiserver.go:172`, `apiserver.go:163-176`)

```go
featureGateArgs, err := cfg.ApiServer.FeatureGates.ToApiserverArgs()
// ToApiserverArgs does NOT check FeatureSet. It unconditionally processes:
addFeatures(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled, true)  // → "SomeFeature=true"
```

Then at line 235:

```go
"feature-gates": featureGateArgs,  // → ["SomeFeature=true", "UserNamespacesSupport=true", ...]
```

**Result:** `SomeFeature=true` is passed to kube-apiserver.

summary: CVE-2026-26007 openshift-enterprise-ansible-operator-container: cryptography Subgroup Attack Due to Missing Subgroup Validation for SECT Curves [openshift-4.20]
description:
cryptography Subgroup Attack Due to Missing Subgroup Validation for SECT Curves

cryptography is a package designed to expose cryptographic primitives and recipes to Python developers. Prior to 46.0.5, the public_key_from_numbers (or EllipticCurvePublicNumbers.public_key()), EllipticCurvePublicNumbers.public_key(), load_der_public_key() and load_pem_public_key() functions do not verify that the point belongs to the expected prime-order subgroup of the curve. This missing validation allows an attacker to provide a public key point P from a small-order subgroup. This can lead to security issues in various situations, such as the most commonly used signature verification (ECDSA) and shared key negotiation (ECDH). When the victim computes the shared secret as S = \[victim_private_key\]P via ECDH, this leaks information about victim_private_key mod (small_subgroup_order). For curves with cofactor > 1, this reveals the least significant bits of the private key. When these weak public keys are used in ECDSA , it's easy to forge signatures on the small subgroup. Only SECT curves are impacted by this. This vulnerability is fixed in 46.0.5.  
\--   
Image `registry.redhat.io/openshift4/ose-ansible-rhel9-operator:v4.20` uses vulnerable version `cryptography==41.0.7`

If the bootstrap VM fails to start it’s impossible to recover its logs

summary: Our customer has reported the following issues to Red Hat Support that they have encountered after a CNI migration from OpenShiftSDN to OVNKubernetes:
description:
Case 04393258  
Problem: For the namespace in which egress is assigned, after the migration internal traffic for namespace (internal to namespace and internal to cluster) is not working. When they remove the egress IP and add it back, connectivity starts working as expected. However, the problem resurfaces after working for some time. Customer has to remove and reattach the EgressIPs to make the connectivity work again. Clusters currently running 4.16.30 and 4.16.36.

Case 04390281  
Problem: Vault and external connectivity for the customer broke after the SDN to OVN upgrade for their cluster namicggtd30d. They mentioned that SMCP is present for this project. They discovered that the root cause was not EgressIP, but rather how the services were binding. Specifically, the pods could not bind to the same port simultaneously; when one pod bound to a NodePort, the second pod would fail. The RH Support Engineer resolved this by creating an additional service and exposing a separate NodePort. According to Citi, while it remains unclear why this functioned with a single port under SDN but requires two under OVN, they feel that SDN and OVN typically should not affect port bindings other than the default port changes used for encapsulation (e.g., VXLAN vs. Geneve). They want to know the key differences between SDN and OVN and what is causing these inherent issues when they migrate. They want a comprehensive document about pre-steps and post-steps they can take with the OVN migration to prevent these issues from occurring

Case 04387488  
Problem: Application was down and returned the error 'This site can't be reached' after the migration. Also, the application's vanity URL and other routes were not working. Upon checking, they noticed the network policy "allow-from-openshift-ingress" was missing in the namespace. Once they added back the network policy 'allow-from-openshift-ingress' to the project, the application started to work properly. They need to understand that how the network policy 'allow-from-openshift-ingress' got deleted. please check and advise.

Case 04393258  
Problem: After migration from SDN to OVN on Cluster namicggtd51d, client is not able to access a specific URL from inside the Pod in their namespace, which was working fine before the migration. One thing they noticed is that the issue gets fixed after they remove and add back the Egress IP assigned to the Namespace. But after sometime the issue happens again and they have to reattach the EgressIPs for the connectivity to work again (possibly related to Case 04393258.

We are not sure if any of this is related to the bugs that were fixed with version 4.16.53. The customer will upgrade to this version if Red Hat requires them to. However, considering that they have 600+ clusters to upgrade, they want to be sure that the upgrade will indeed fix their issues as it is not a trivial task for them to do the upgrades on such a large farm of clusters. If some of these issues are not related t

summary: Update Release Configs for event-ttl GA
Update KAS-O for event-TTL GA
MCO cleanup removes locally created initramfs-etc tracking added via a custom service, which causes NBDE Tang decryption to fail on reboot in DHCP-less environments.
description:
In DHCP-less setups using the Agent-Based Installer (ABI) with Network-Bound Disk Encryption (NBDE), static network configurations are not automatically included in the initramfs of the target system. Since early boot requires network access to the Tang server for disk decryption, administrators must manually ensure that NetworkManager and DNS configurations are tracked:

```
rpm-ostree initramfs-etc --track /etc/NetworkManager --track /etc/resolv.conf
```

To automate this, the customer implemented a MachineConfig that deploys a one-time systemd service (`initramfs-track.service`) to run this command during the first boot.

**The Issue:**  
The service runs successfully on the first boot and creates a new local rpm-ostree deployment containing the required network configuration. However, this deployment is not recognized by the Machine Config Operator (MCO) as part of the desired system state.

When the MCO later performs its routine cleanup, it removes this locally created deployment. As a result, the initramfs network configuration is lost without any explicit changes or upgrades. On the next reboot, the node cannot reach the Tang server and fails to decrypt the disks.

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1.  Deploy an OpenShift cluster using ABI with NBDE in a DHCP-less environment (static IPs defined in `agent-config.yaml`). 
2. Apply a MachineConfig that installs a one-shot systemd service to run:

    ```
    rpm-ostree initramfs-etc --track /etc/NetworkManager --track /etc/resolv.conf
    ```

    on first boot.  
      
    `cat /etc/systemd/system/initramfs-track.service`



    `[Unit]`



    `Description=Generate initramfs overlay via rpm-ostree`



    `After=local-fs.target`



    `Before=multi-user.target`



    



    `[Service]`



    `Type=oneshot`



    `ExecStart=/usr/bin/rpm-ostree initramfs-etc --track /etc/NetworkManager --track /etc/resolv.conf`



    `ExecStartPost=/bin/systemctl disable initramfs-track.service`



    `RemainAfterExit=yes`



    



    `[Install]`



    `WantedBy=multi-user.target`


3.  Confirm that a new deployment is staged and the overlay is present under `/boot/ostree/`. 
4.  Allow the node to continue running until the MCO performs a cleanup (or trigger it via a MachineConfig update). 
5.  Run `rpm-ostree status -v` on the node. 

**Actual Results:**

*  The tracked files are no longer present in the rpm-ostree state. 
*  The overlay directory under `/boot/ostree/` is removed. 
* System logs show MCO-triggered cleanup removing the deployment:

    ```
    rpm-ostree[...] Initiated txn Cleanup for client(id:machine-config-operator ...)
    ```
*  After reboot, the node fails to decrypt disks due to missing network configuration required to reach the Tang server. 

**Expected Results:**

* Changes introduced via `rpm-ostree initramfs-etc --track` should persist across MCO reconciliation and cleanup operations.

summary: Dual-stack cluster: Node with link-local IPv6 prevents new nodes from joining and destabilizes OVN-Kubernetes
description:
**Description of problem:**

When adding new nodes to a dual-stack cluster, if an existing node has an invalid IPv6 configuration (link-local only), it blocks newly added nodes from becoming Ready.

Additionally, this leads to instability in OVN-Kubernetes, where ovnkube-node pods on control-plane nodes enter CrashLoopBackOff.

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

OpenShift Version: 4.x

**How reproducible:**

**Steps to Reproduce:**  
1.Add a node with IPv6 configured as link-local only in a dual-stack (IPv4/IPv6) cluster  
2.Approve the node certificates → node remains NotReady with warning:

```
k8s.ovn.org/node-chassis-id annotation not found
```

3\. Add another node with correct network configuration and approve certificates

**Actual results:**

* Newly added node remains in NotReady state
* Healthy node provisioning is blocked until the faulty node is fixed
* ovnkube-node pods on control-plane nodes enter CrashLoopBackOff
* Cluster networking becomes unstable

**Expected results:**

* Node with invalid IPv6 configuration should remain NotReady
* Newly added nodes with correct configuration should become Ready
* ovnkube-node pods should remain stable (no crashes)
* Faulty node should not impact other nodes joining the cluster

**Additional info:**

* Once the faulty node’s IP configuration is corrected, all nodes transition to Ready
* Observed CrashLoopBackOff in ovnkube-controller container on master nodes
* Sample issue seen in pod:

```
Reason: CrashLoopBackOff
failed to run ovnkube
```

* Node states confirm dependency on fixing the faulty node before others recover

---

Please fill in the following template while reporting a bug and provide as much relevant information as possible.    
Doing so will give us the best chance to find a prompt resolution.

---

### Affected Platforms

Is it an:

* \[ \] internal CI failure  
* \[ \] customer issue / SD  
* \[ \] internal Red Hat testing failure  

---

### If it is an internal Red Hat testing failure

* Please share a kubeconfig or creds to a live cluster for the assignee to debug/troubleshoot along with reproducer steps    
  (especially if it's a telco use case like ICNI, secondary bridges or BM + kubevirt).

---

### If it is a CI failure

* Did it happen in different CI lanes? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* Did it happen in both SDN and OVN jobs? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* Did it happen on other platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP, Bare Metal)? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* When did the failure start happening? Please provide the **UTC timestamp** of the networking outage window from a sample failure run.  
* If it's a connectivity issue, please provide:

    * srcNode, srcIP, srcNamespace, and srcPodName  
    * dstNode, dstIP, dstNamespace, and dstPodName  
    * traffic path (examples: pod→pod, po

summary: [4.19] UDN service flows on br-ex are not correctly considering ip family
description:
Two issues have been observed with some of the (C)UDN service flows 

* At least one UDN service flow relies on the ClusterIP field of the service instead of the ClusterIPs field

    ```
    flows := []string{fmt.Sprintf("cookie=%s, priority=300, table=2, %s, %s_dst=%s, "+
    				"actions=set_field:%s->eth_dst,output:%s",
    				defaultOpenFlowCookie, ipPrefix, ipPrefix, service.Spec.ClusterIP,
    				npw.ofm.getDefaultBridgeMAC().String(), defaultNetConfig.ofPortPatch)}
    ```
* At least one UDN service flow hardcodes a field on IPv4 family

    ```
    flows = append(flows, fmt.Sprintf("cookie=%s, priority=490, in_port=%s, ip, ip_src=%s,actions=ct(zone=%d,nat,table=3)",
    					defaultOpenFlowCookie, defaultNetConfig.ofPortPatch, service.Spec.ClusterIP, config.Default.HostMasqConntrackZone))
    ```

For the first issue, no actual consequence has been observed although it is expected that traffic flows to the non-preferred IP family of the KAPI/DNS services (so IPv6 on dual stack) will fail.

The second issue has been observed on a single stack IPv6 cluster causing the complete flow sync to fail permanently when a CUDN is advertised, making traffic flows CUDN<->external to fail (and potentially impacting any further networking change afterwards):

```

summary: OpenShift Installer Does Not Set Explicit maxUnavailable in MachineConfigPools
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
OpenShift installer creates MachineConfigPools (both master and worker) without explicitly setting the spec.maxUnavailable field, relying on implicit defaults. This causes Red Hat Insights to flag fresh out-of-the-box (OOTB) clusters with Risk Level 2 warnings, creating confusion for administrators who expect OOTB configurations to comply with Red Hat's own best practices.
                                                                                                                                                                                              
The implicit default (maxUnavailable: 1) is functionally correct, especially for master pools with etcd quorum requirements, but the lack of explicit configuration:                        
- Triggers false-positive Insights recommendations (rule: CCX_RULES_OCP.EXTERNAL.RULES.MCP_UNAVAILABLE_MACHINE_COUNT)
- Creates operational visibility gaps (admins cannot see actual behavior without reading documentation)                                                                                     
- Contradicts the principle of explicit configuration for safety-critical parameters                                                                                                        
- Causes confusion: "Why is Red Hat warning about Red Hat's own defaults?"                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                              
This is a product quality issue affecting all IPI-installed clusters. 
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
OpenShift Container Platform 4.20.14, 4.20.15                                                                                                                                               
Likely affects all 4.x versions
```

**How reproducible:**

```
100% - Verified on multiple clusters across different OpenShift versions
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
  1. Install fresh OpenShift 4.20.x cluster using IPI method on AWS                                                                                                                           
  2. Check MachineConfigPool configuration:
     oc get machineconfigpool master worker -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, maxUnavailable: .spec.maxUnavailable}'                                                            
  3. Verify both master and worker MCPs show "maxUnavailable: null"                                                                                                                           
  4. Wait for cluster nodes to reboot (or wait for scheduled Insights data collection)                                                                                                        
  5. Check Insights

summary: [4.18] - Application pods traffic is dropped during the kube-apiserver rollout
description:
Description of problem: Application pod traffic is dropped due to kube-apiserver rollout events

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable): 4.18.23

Summary:

* Customer has etcd encyption enabled in the cluster and hence due to that kube-apiserver pod rolls out every week.
* Due to rollout customer's application pod traffic is dropped.

summary: Component Readiness: [Machine Config Operator] [Other]  PinnedImages GC test image cleanup issue
description:
\[Generated AI Summary\]                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
1\. Test creates a custom MachineConfigPool                                                                                                                                                                         

The PinnedImages test (\[sig-mco\]\[OCPFeatureGate:PinnedImages\] All Nodes in a custom Pool should have the PinnedImages even after Garbage Collection) moves a worker node into a custom MCP. This causes MCO to       
render a new config for that pool — hence rendered-custom-<hash>.                                                                                                                                                  

2\. Worker node references the custom rendered config                                                                                                                                                               

The affected node (always one of the workers — worker-b, worker-a, etc.) gets assigned rendered-custom-<hash> as its desired MachineConfig while in the custom pool.                                               

3\. Test cleanup deletes the custom MCP — but the node still references the old config                                                                                                                              

When the test cleans up and deletes the custom MachineConfigPool, the rendered-custom-<hash> MachineConfig object is also removed. However, the node still has an annotation referencing that rendered config. The   
node's MCD (Machine Config Daemon) then tries to reconcile against a config that no longer exists.

4\. Node gets stuck in a degraded state                                                                                                                                                                             

From the verify operator conditions machine-config error:                                                                                                                                                          

MachineConfigPool worker is not ready                                                                                                                                                                                
total: 3, ready: 2, updated: 2, unavailable: 1                                                                                                                                                                     
Node worker-b reporting: "machineconfig rendered-custom-<hash> not found"                                                                                                                                        

The node can't complete its config sync because the target config doesn't exist. It:

description:
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-77252#icft=OCPBUGS-77252</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-77055#icft=OCPBUGS-77055</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
**Description of problem:**

In <=4.20, there exists a [machine count conditional](https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/blob/53228343a48901cbdcc3a159c5c77e4e47e2ca13/pkg/controller/node/status.go#L210-L224) that has caused some confusion in the MCN GA and bug fixing efforts. While it's accuracy and purpose has been questioned in a few occasions, the conditional has remained due to its historical performance and to keep the scope of changes appropriately sized. This bug should aim to update the main \`calculateStatus\` function to be clearer.

Note that the \`calculateStatus\` function was refactored slightly in 4.21, so this bug only pertains to <=4.20.

summary: Set Ceph noout Flag During SDN-to-OVN Migration to Prevent Unnecessary Recovery
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
During the planned SDN-to-OVN migration, a rolling reboot of all nodes will be performed. This can trigger unnecessary Ceph recovery operations if not handled properly.

As highlighted in internal communication, clusters running ODF must have the Ceph noout flag set before starting the migration and unset only after all nodes have successfully rejoined and Ceph reports a healthy status.

This step is critical to avoid unnecessary data rebalancing and recovery during node restarts.

Steps to Perform:

Before starting SDN-to-OVN migration, execute:

ceph osd set noout
Proceed with rolling migration/reboots.

After all nodes are back online and cluster status is HEALTH_OK, execute:

ceph osd unset noout
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Do not set noout flag before migration for a cluster with ODF.
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Ensure a cluster is deployed with ODF (Ceph) and is in healthy state (HEALTH_OK).
2. Do not set the Ceph noout flag.
3. Initiate SDN-to-OVN migration which triggers a rolling reboot of nodes.
4. Observe the cluster during node restarts:
   - Nodes go down one by one.
   - Ceph detects OSDs as temporarily unavailable.

5. Check Ceph cluster status during the process:
   - ceph -s
6. Observe that Ceph begins recovery/backfilling/rebalancing operations due to perceived OSD failures.
7. Continue until all nodes reboot and rejoin the cluster.
```

**Actual results:**

```
Unstable cluster after the node restart.
```

**Expected results:**

```
No unnecessary Ceph recovery/rebalancing during migration.
Stable cluster behavior throughout node restarts.
```

**Additional info:**

```
KCS: 6495631 (Ceph noout usage guidance)
Support Case: 04373200
```

summary: MCS cannot work with newer versions of Ignition
description:
When Ignition requests config from MCS, it passes an Accepts header that explicitly includes only the latest version of config it can accept, e.g.

```
application/vnd.coreos.ignition+json;version=3.5.0, */*;q=0.1
```

Prior to <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/MCO-1591</custom> we used to return the latest available version of config if the Accept version was a higher minor version of 3.x.

After that change (in 4.20), we return `400 Bad Request` if we cannot support the _exact_ version identified in the Accepts header. So every time a new version of Ignition is released with support for a new config version, it will be unable to pull config from MCS until MCS is updated to vendor the new release of Ignition.

This happened with [Ignition 2.26](https://github.com/coreos/ignition/blob/main/docs/release-notes.md#ignition-2260-2026-02-17), which introduced config version 3.6.0 (<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-80970</custom>), and will continue to happen with every new config version.

Because CVO upgrades MCO before rolling out the new CoreOS version, upgrades even from affected versions into 4.22 releases don’t appear to be impacted.

summary: ABI installs failing with nodes unable to pull ignition
description:
We have a new failure in agent-based installs in 4.22 CI. After rebooting the hosts for them to come up as a node, we see this error from Ignition:

```
GET https://192.168.111.5:22623/config/master: attempt #1
GET result: Bad Request
failed to fetch config: failed to fetch resource
failed to acquire config: failed to fetch resource
Ignition failed: failed to fetch resource
```

on every node, every time.

The first versions we see failures on are:

* [https://amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org/releasestream/4.22.0-0.ci/release/4.22.0-0.ci-2026-03-26-100505](https://amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org/releasestream/4.22.0-0.ci/release/4.22.0-0.ci-2026-03-26-100505)
* [https://amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org/releasestream/4.22.0-0.nightly/release/4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-03-26-163214](https://amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org/releasestream/4.22.0-0.nightly/release/4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-03-26-163214)

Coincidentally these are the first versions to upgrade from RHCOS [9.8.20260324-0](https://releases-rhcos--prod-pipeline.apps.int.prod-stable-spoke1-dc-iad2.itup.redhat.com/?arch=x86_64&release=9.8.20260324-0&stream=prod%2Fstreams%2Frhel-9.8#9.8.20260324-0) to [9.8.20260325-1](https://releases-rhcos--prod-pipeline.apps.int.prod-stable-spoke1-dc-iad2.itup.redhat.com/?arch=x86_64&release=9.8.20260325-1&stream=prod%2Fstreams%2Frhel-9.8#9.8.20260325-1) (which updates Ignition to `2.26.0-1.el9_8`).

The [release notes for Ignition 2.26](https://github.com/coreos/ignition/blob/main/docs/release-notes.md#ignition-2260-2026-02-17) look extremely benign, and yet the coincidence is quite striking.

summary: OVN-Kubernetes: survivor local SB missing remote peer after same-name control-plane replace (east–west broken)
description:
## Description of problem

When a control-plane node is reprovisioned while in the cluster (e.g., Ironic reprovisioning in baremetal/VM environments), the new chassis-id conflicts with the existing chassis-id in the node object. OVN-Kubernetes cannot override the chassis-id in the node object, preventing recovery and leaving the cluster in a broken state with no east-west pod traffic.

Symptom: After same-name control-plane node replacement, east-west pod traffic fails with **100% packet loss** between pod subnets across nodes, **\`dial tcp <pod-on-peer>:8443: i/o timeout\`** from `kube-apiserver` to aggregated API backends, **503** / flaky **APIService** checks.

## Version-Release number

4.22

## How reproducible

Always (when control-plane node is reprovisioned while in cluster, creating chassis-id conflict)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Deploy TNF cluster with baremetal platform using VMs (TNF/dev-scripts style)
2. Trigger Ironic reprovisioning of a control-plane node (e.g., `master-0`) while it's still in the cluster
3. New chassis-id is generated during reprovisioning
4. Observe OVN-Kubernetes attempting to update node object with new chassis-id
5. Update fails due to existing chassis-id in node object
6. Attempt pod-to-pod communication across nodes

## Actual results

* OVN-Kubernetes cannot override chassis-id in node object
* OVNK enters retry/crash loop trying to write updated chassis metadata
* Cluster recovery blocked
* 100% packet loss between pod subnets across nodes
* East-west pod traffic broken
* Underlay ICMP (node-to-node) still works

## Expected results

* OVN-Kubernetes should be able to override chassis-id in node object when it changes
* Chassis-id should update successfully when Ironic reprovisions a node
* Both nodes should converge to consistent state automatically
* East-west pod traffic should recover without manual intervention

## Additional info

**Root cause:**

* OVNK cannot override chassis-id annotation (`k8s.ovn.org/node-chassis-id`) in node object when it changes
* This prevents recovery after reprovisioning
* Cluster is left in broken state until manual intervention

**Proposed fix:**

* Allow node-object to overwrite chassis-id when it changes (like when Ironic reprovisions a node that was in the cluster)
* Enable OVNK to update node chassis identity without entering permanent retry/crash loop

**Workarounds:**

* Delete the node object completely (forces clean join path)
* Restart `ovnkube-node` on both nodes to force resync (even after node delete)
* TNF NR test: `recoverOVNKForNodeReplacement` restarts `ovnkube-node` on both nodes after replacement

**Related:** OCPBUGS-80957, OCPBUGS-80958 (same-name node replacement issues in other operators)

summary: The default service account in the CVO namespace should not have cluster-admin permissions.
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/pull/1266 adds 
bespoke service accounts to cluster-version-operator- and version- pods.
However, it also revealed a bug in the CVO logic; using delete annotation
`"release.openshift.io/delete": "true"` does not in fact delete artifacts
after upgrades. This has left us with no choice but to keep a ClusterRoleBinding
for default service account to give it cluster-admin permissions. 

The mentioned pods above will not be using default service account in 4.22+ , 
but the CRB has to remain in order to allow upgrade tests to run successfully. 

This means, however, that in 4.22+ , the default service account in
cluster-version-operator namespace will have cluster-admin permissions in the
core payload, even if no current pods are using it.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
‌
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. 
2. 
3. 
```

**Actual results:**

```
default service account in cluster-version-operator namespace has 
cluster-admin permissions in order to ensure upgrade tests are passing.
```

**Expected results:**

```
default service account in cluster-version-operator namespace should not
have any additional permissions, and the upgrade tests should still pass.
```

**Additional info:**

```
See the discussion in 
https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/pull/1266 to understand
better why the implementation had to be done in this way initially.
```

summary: Unexpected failover triggered by API/etcd latency during must-gather execution
description:
**Description of problem:**

The customer maintains a high-availability (HA) environment using an active-standby failover mechanism that monitors custom annotations on Endpoint objects.

During a routine must-gather diagnostic collection, the following issues occurred:

* Latency Spike: API request duration exceeded 20 seconds, accompanied by a significant increase in etcd request latency.
* System Impact: This latency disrupted the failover heartbeat, leading the system to incorrectly identify a failure and trigger an unintended failover.

Goals:

* Identify the root cause of the API/etcd latency during must-gather.
* Provide guidance on mitigating such spikes to prevent impact on latency-sensitive failover mechanisms.

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
‌
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Execute the following must-gather command.

```
oc adm must-gather \
   --image-stream=openshift/must-gather \
   --image=quay.io/pg.next/pg-must-gather \
   `csvJson=$(oc get clusterserviceversions.operators.coreos.com -o json); for csv_name in $(echo "$csvJson" | jq '.items[].metadata.name'); do \
      mgImage=$(echo "$csvJson" | jq '.items[] | select (.metadata.name == '$csv_name') | .spec |  select (.relatedImages != null).relatedImages[] | select ((.name | contains ("must-gather")) or (.name | contains ("cluster-logging-operator"))) | .image' | sed 's/\"//g'); \
      if [[ ! $mgImage == "" ]]; then \
         echo "--image=$mgImage"; \
      fi; \
   done` \
   | tee must-gather-console.log
```

**Actual results:**

```
‌
```

**Expected results:**

```
‌
```

**Additional info:**

```
‌
```

Please fill in the following template while reporting a bug and provide as much relevant information as possible.    
Doing so will give us the best chance to find a prompt resolution.

---

### Affected Platforms

Is it an:

* \[ \] internal CI failure  
* \[ \] customer issue / SD  
* \[ \] internal Red Hat testing failure  

---

### If it is an internal Red Hat testing failure

* Please share a kubeconfig or creds to a live cluster for the assignee to debug/troubleshoot along with reproducer steps    
  (especially if it's a telco use case like ICNI, secondary bridges or BM + kubevirt).

---

### If it is a CI failure

* Did it happen in different CI lanes? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* Did it happen in both SDN and OVN jobs? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* Did it happen on other platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP, Bare Metal)? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* When did the failure start happening? Please provide the **UTC timestamp** of the networking outage window from a sample failure run.  
* If it's a connectivity issue, please provide:

    * srcNode, srcIP, srcNamespace, and srcPodName  
    * dstNode, dstIP, dstNamespace, and dstPodName  
    * traffic path (examples: pod→pod, pod→external, pod→svc, pod→node, etc.)
    

---

### If it is a Customer / SD issue

* Provide enough information in the bug description that Engineering doesn’t need to read the entire case history.  
* Don’t presume that Engineering has access to Salesforce.  
* Do presume that Engineering will access attachments through supportshell.  
* Describ

summary: Extreme CPU Usage observed from Kubernetes components due to golang-fips TLS1.3 handling
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
Customer is seeing huge CPU usage on the kube-apiserver after upgrading to OCP 4.20.

Customer managed to get a pprof from an API server as this occurred 
and found that 60%+ of the time was being spent handling TLS handshakes with the following call tree:

runtime.cgocall                                    164.63s  61.05%
  crypto/tls.(*Conn).HandshakeContext              161.88s  60.03%
    crypto/tls.(*serverHandshakeStateTLS13).handshake  148.86s  55.20%
      crypto/tls.expandLabel                       154.47s  57.28%
        io.ReadAll                                 153.35s  56.86%
          golang-fips/openssl/v2.(*hkdf).Read
            _Cfunc_go_openssl_EVP_PKEY_derive_wrapper 

Customer noticed that compiling the API server using https://github.com/golang-fips/go and 
believes the root cause of the issues is due the expandLabel handling in there, specifically 
this looks to be very expensive: https://github.com/golang-fips/go/blob/f9990dae1ad17df49fe7ec70a707e764fc3c0004/patches/000-fips.patch#L4694-L4699 
when tracing the code due to the number of times expandLabel is called in a handshake and the 
implementation of that between the IO handling and the reader.

This started to happen in 4.20 and customer's assumption is because images were upgraded 
to be compiled with Go 1.24 which has a much different handling of expandLabel in 1.24 vs 1.23

This is causing huge impact on the stability of the cluster.

Customer also sees that this issue is reproducible when shutting down one kube-apiserver,
the same behavior starts on the other kube-apiservers.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
OpenShift Container Platfrom 4.20.5
With enabled FIPS
```

**How reproducible:**

```
n/a - at customer's site
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. 
2. 
3. 
```

**Actual results:**

```
‌
```

**Expected results:**

```
‌
```

**Additional info:**

```

description:

h3. Test Name
