_Capability:_ Other  
_Environment:_ Architecture:arm64 | FeatureSet:default | Installer:ipi | Network:ovn | Platform:azure | Topology:ha | Upgrade:none  
_Regression Opened:_ 2026-03-24  

---

h3. Overview

The _pod-network-availability collection_ monitor test is experiencing an _extreme regression_ on 4.22 (arm64, Azure, OVN, HA, IPI). This test monitors pod network availability by running a disruption poller workload and collecting results. The test pass rate has dropped from _100.00% (4.21 baseline)_ to _78.57% (4.22 sample)_, a statistically significant drop confirmed by a Fisher's Exact regression probability of 99.99%.

_Regression Analysis:_

* Extreme regression detected.
* Fisher's Exact probability of a regression: 99.99%.
* Test pass rate dropped from 100.00% to 78.57%.

In all three observed failures, the disruption poller pod(s) ({{host-network-to-pod-network-disruption-poller-\*}}) are stuck in {{ContainerCreating}} state, preventing the collection phase from completing. The companion cleanup test also fails in all three runs with a {{context deadline exceeded}} error from the client rate limiter, indicating the stuck pods are blocking cleanup as well.

summary: [release-4.20] EgressFirewall not recognize FQDN DNS related IP change on time
description:
**Description of problem:**

After define a EgressFirewall like below to only allow egress access for dnsName: xxxxx

, when dnsName related IP changes (like AWS NLB recreate) suddenly,  EgressFirewall resolves the IP very slowly ( can be several min \~ 30 min ), while from the application pod, the DNS resolution is very fast.

synchronization gap between the application's DNS resolution and the firewall controller's update cycle exists, which needs to be fixed

```java
apiVersion: k8s.ovn.org/v1
kind: EgressFirewall
metadata:
  name: default
spec:
  egress:
  - to:
      dnsName: hello-dns-ttl-test.apps.jacobtest7.79qs.p1.openshiftapps.com
    type: Allow
  - to:
      cidrSelector: 0.0.0.0/0 
    type: Deny
```

Detail discusstion here:

[https://redhat-internal.slack.com/archives/C0A9VH51J01/p1768813281179229](https://redhat-internal.slack.com/archives/C0A9VH51J01/p1768813281179229)

 

**How reproducible:**

1) Create one application to expose the FQDN (hello-dns-ttl-test.apps.jacobtest7.79qs.p1.openshiftapps.com) in ROSA openshift cluster 4.17.43 using the default Ingress Controller's domain name.

 

```java
$ oc new-project dns-ttl-test
$ oc new-app --name hello \
  --image=registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/httpd-24
$ oc expose svc/hello
route/hello exposed
$ oc get route hello -n dns-ttl-test
NAME    HOST/PORT                                                      PATH   SERVICES   PORT       TERMINATION   WILDCARD

$ oc get pods
NAME                                  READY   STATUS        RESTARTS   AGE

$ oc get svc -n openshift-ingress

router-internal-default   ClusterIP      172.30.161.253   <none>                                                                          80/TCP,443/TCP,1936/TCP      91m

summary: AWS security group shows 2 different ovn port 6441-6442 & 6641-6642 rules for two different cluster versions
description:
**Description of problem:** 

* Customer is having 2 AWS IPI clusters and ocp-prd1 is on 4.19 & ocp-tst2 on 4.20
* Both are having 2 rules for a SecurityGroup :

    * On the ocp-prd1 cluster we have 2 rules for port 6641-6642 that allows traffic between workers and the control plane.
    * On the ocp-tst2 cluster we have 2 rules for port 6441-6442 that allows traffic between workers and the control plane.
    
* But as per OVN upstream [Documentation](https://docs.ovn.org/en/latest/howto/firewalld.html)  6641-6642 is ovn northbound and southbound databases ports and the cluster show different port rules which are conflicting to documentation.

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**  

```
OCP 4.19 & OCP 4.20
```

‌

**How reproducible:**

```
Install 2 4.19 & 4.20 clusters with IPI method based installation on AWS. 
```

‌

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. ```
  Once Clusters are installed navigate to EC2 Instance --> Security Group --> Select the associated group --> go to Inbound rules section --> Search for 6441/6641 
  ```

**Actual results:**

* While I tested the rules for 4.20 version the rule observed was 6441-6442 which is not the same the customer has got and also not the standard ovn port which docs says. 

    * Refer to  atpatil-secGroup-4.20
    

**Expected results:**

* The both cluster versions should show the OVN documentation defined port i.e. 6641-6642.

**Additional info:**

* Customer's 4.19 security group shows 6441-6442 

    * Refer to attachment Prd-SecGroup.png
    
* Customer's 4.20 security group shows 6641-6642 

    * Refer to attachment Tst-GecGroup.png + tst-SecGroup-2.png

summary: MCO Boot Image Controller attempts to downgrade RHCOS 10 to RHCOS 9 when osImageStream is set to rhel-10
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
  When a cluster is installed with osImageStream: rhel-10 in install-config.yaml, the MCO Boot Image Controller attempts to
  downgrade worker node boot images from RHCOS 10.2 to RHCOS 9.6, even though the installer correctly created machines with RHCOS 10
  images.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.22.0
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
  1. Create install-config.yaml with:
  osImageStream: rhel-10
  featureSet: TechPreviewNoUpgrade
  
  2. Install OpenShift cluster on GCP

  3. Check machine-config-controller logs for boot image reconciliation

  I0324 02:32:24.182561 platform_helpers.go:74] Reconciling MAPI machineset ci-op-tvyyxyp6-cc354-9qx4f-worker-f on GCP, with arch
  x86_64
  I0324 02:32:24.183655 platform_helpers.go:129] New target boot image:
  projects/rhcos-cloud/global/images/rhcos-9-6-20251023-0-gcp-x86-64
  I0324 02:32:24.183670 platform_helpers.go:130] Current image: projects/rhcos-cloud/global/images/rhcos-10-2-20260217-0-gcp-x86-64
```

**Actual results:**

```
The boot image specified in the worker machineset was updated to rhcos-9.
```

**Expected results:**

```
  - MCO should skip boot image updates for RHEL 10 MachineSets on GCP
  - Boot image should remain RHCOS 10.2
```

**Additional info:**

```
Reference test job artifacts: https://gcsweb-qe-private-deck-ci.apps.ci.l2s4.p1.openshiftapps.com/gcs/qe-private-deck/pr-logs/pull/openshift_release/76670/rehearse-76670-periodic-ci-openshift-verification-tests-main-installation-nightly-4.22-gcp-ipi-cco-manual-users-static-mini-perm-f28/2036239347618942976/artifacts/gcp-ipi-cco-manual-users-static-mini-perm-f28/gather-extra/artifacts/
```

summary: Hypershift e2e tests fail after AutoSizingReserved enabled
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
After AutoSizingReserved was enabled by default in OpenShift 4.21, Hypershift e2e tests fail because platform pods (monitoring, DNS, networking, registry) cannot be scheduled on hosted cluster worker nodes due to insufficient allocatable memory. AutoSizingReserved dynamically increases system-reserved memory based on node size, which significantly reduces the memory available to pods on the small instance types (m5.large, m5.xlarge) used by the e2e tests.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
OpenShift 4.21
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
Enable AutoSizingReserved by default (NODE_SIZING_ENABLED=true) for all topologies, including Hypershift hosted clusters (ControlPlaneTopology=External).
Run Hypershift e2e tests that create hosted clusters with m5.xlarge (~8 GiB) or m5.large (~4 GiB) worker nodes.
Observe that platform pods in the hosted cluster remain Pending with FailedScheduling events. 
```

**Actual results:**

```
AutoSizingReserved increases system-reserved memory, reducing allocatable memory because of which pods (especially monitoring/Prometheus) cannot be scheduled. Cluster Operators (monitoring, etc.) degrade, and e2e tests fail.
```

**Expected results:**

```
Hypershift e2e tests should pass with AutoSizingReserved enabled. Worker nodes should have sufficient allocatable memory for all platform pods to schedule and run successfully.
```

**Additional info:**

```
‌
```

summary: Pod stuck in podinitializing state and endpoint is stuck in notReadyAddresses but after deleting the endpoint the pod comes back up
description:
**Description of problem:**

Pods gets stuck in podinitializing state and endpoint is stuck in notReadyAddresses but after deleting the endpoint the pod comes back up

* While the pods are being injected with consul
* As Per Consul:  

    ```
    Although the visible error appears in the Consul connect-init container (Unable to find registered services; retrying), the behavior suggests a timing issue around how the Kubernetes Service/Endpoints state is observed during pod startup.
    What we’re seeing:
     
    •	The Pod schedules successfully.
    •	The Service exists.
    •	During connect-init execution, the injector does not observe the expected service endpoint.
    •	Deleting or recreating the Endpoints object consistently resolves the issue without changing the Pod or Consul configuration.
     
    This indicates that the correct endpoint state can be computed, but in some cases it is not observed by the injector during the initial reconciliation window.
    ```

So far from logs, etcd performance and everything looks fine which is weird.

summary: 4.20- Custom serviceAccountIssuer unsets service-account-jwks-uri
description:
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-78548#icft=OCPBUGS-78548</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
Description of problem:

```java
When a custom serviceAccountIssuer is set on the Authentication CR, the service-account-jwks-uri argument in the KubeAPIServer CR will no longer be set to https://<API_URL>/openid/v1/jwks. 

This means that the jwks_uri field from the /.well-known/openid-configurations endpoint will now be set to https://<MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443/openid/v1/jwks. The problem is that even if the client machine is configured to trust the master node's certificate CA, there will still be an error because the certificate's SAN does not include the master node's IP.
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
4.17.6
```

How reproducible:

```java
This is a reproducible issue
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```java
1. Patch the authentication CR with a custom serviceAccountIssuer.
~~~
oc patch authentication cluster --type merge --patch '{"spec": {"serviceAccountIssuer": "https://test.com"}}'
~~~

2. Wait for any rollouts to complete as seen with oc get co. If any pods go into CrashLoopBackOff then delete them with this command:
~~~
oc get pods --all-namespaces | grep CrashLoopBackOff | awk '{print "--namespace " $1 " " $2}' | xargs -L 1 oc delete pod
~~~

3. Check the value of jwks_uri from the well-known endpoint:
~~~
oc get --raw /.well-known/openid-configuration | jq -r '.jwks_uri'
https://<MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443/openid/v1/jwks
~~~     

4. Trust the master node certificate CA which appears to be kube-apiserver-service-network-signer by default.
~~~
oc extract secret/service-network-serving-signer -n openshift-kube-apiserver-operator

sudo cp tls.crt /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/tls.crt
sudo update-ca-trust
~~~

5. Curl the master node IP.
~~~
curl https://<MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443
curl: (60) SSL: no alternative certificate subject name matches target host name '<MASTER_NODE_IP>'
~~~

6. Check the SAN of the master node's certificate.
~~~
openssl s_client -connect <MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443 -showcerts 2>&1 | openssl x509 -text | grep 'Subject Alternative Name' -A 1
            X509v3 Subject Alternative Name: 
                DNS:kubernetes, DNS:kubernetes.default, DNS:kubernetes.default.svc, DNS:kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local, DNS:openshift, DNS:openshift.default, DNS:openshift.default.svc, DNS:openshift.default.svc.cluster.local, DNS:172.30.0.1, IP Address:172.30.0.1
~~~
```

Actual results:

```java
The value of jwks_uri is not accessible with TLS.
```

Expected results:

```java
The value of jwks_uri is accessible with TLS.
```

Additional info:

```java
The customer is not looking for a specific solution so for example, they will be ok with any of the following solutions:
   - Allow for jwks_uri to be configured to a custom value.
   - Add the master node IP to

summary: Virtual machine startup is stuck with error: "Failed to discover live migration status: unexpected live migration state at pods"
description:
Description of problem:

In the live migration status check, the virt-launcher pods of a VM is identified by looking at pods with label \\_vm.kubevirt.io/name: .\\_ OpenShift Virtualisation automatically sets this label on the virt-launcher pod and by default to VM name. But this label value can be overridden by passing ".spec.template.spec.hostname" in the VM spec. Refer [sanitize.go#L33](https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/blob/main/pkg/util/net/dns/sanitize.go#L33) and [template.go#L1572](https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/blob/6ce87785f2e1d8c326816cbafe93f9049074fe1a/pkg/virt-controller/services/template.go#L1572)

For example, the following VM has the hostname 'test-vm',  the virt-launcher pod is automatically labeled with 'vm.kubevirt.io/name: test-vm':

```java

# oc get vm rhel9-azure-earthworm-57 -o json |jq '.spec.template.spec.hostname'

"test-vm"


# oc get pod virt-launcher-rhel9-azure-earthworm-57-6ddr6 -o json|jq '.metadata.labels'
{
  "kubevirt.io": "virt-launcher",
  "kubevirt.io/created-by": "ffc3ae7a-e048-4032-bddd-6399fb1ec5af",
  "kubevirt.io/domain": "rhel9-azure-earthworm-57",
  "kubevirt.io/nodeName": "10.74.128.230",
  "kubevirt.io/size": "small",
  "network.kubevirt.io/headlessService": "headless",
  "vm.kubevirt.io/name": "test-vm"                     <===
}

```

So the label value may not be unique across all virtual machines.

If there is another VM in the namespace which have the same spec.hostname, the OVN incorrectly finds this VM's virt-launcher pod while looking for "livingPods".  When there are more than 2 VMs with same spec.hostname, the third VM fails to start with tooManyPodsError at [kubevirt/pod.go#L494](https://github.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/blob/2b508cf81c6ad91665f8bc8a82e87b287d232f6c/go-controller/pkg/kubevirt/pod.go#L494)

3 VMs with the same hostname:

```java

# oc get vm -o json | jq -r '.items[] | "\(.metadata.name) \(.spec.template.spec.hostname)"'

rhel9-azure-earthworm-57 test-vm
rhel9-beige-jellyfish-19 test-vm
rhel9-coral-anteater-76 test-vm
```

Out of this, 2 of it was running, and the third one is stuck in starting state:

```java

NAME                       AGE    STATUS     READY
rhel9-azure-earthworm-57   56m    Running    True
rhel9-beige-jellyfish-19   57m    Running    True
rhel9-coral-anteater-76    57m    Starting   False


```

The ovnkube-controller have following error:

```java

I0805 18:29:51.270393    6978 base_network_controller_pods.go:477] [default/nijin-cnv/virt-launcher-rhel9-coral-anteater-76-7qc9m] creating logical port nijin-cnv_virt-launcher-rhel9-coral-anteater-76-7qc9m for pod on switch 18-66-da-9f-a6-de

E0805 18:29:51.285387    6978 obj_retry.go:684] Failed to update *v1.Pod, old=nijin-cnv/virt-launcher-rhel9-coral-anteater-76-7qc9m, new=nijin-cnv/virt-launcher-rhel9-coral-anteater-76-7qc9m, error: failed to discover Live-migration status: unexpected live migration state at pods: nijin-cnv/virt-launcher-rhel9-azure-earthworm-57-6ddr6,nijin-cnv/virt-l

summary: Cluster-version operator should ignore data-plane proxy configuration on HyperShift
description:
**Description of problem**

Since 4.14, HostedCluster has had [_spec.configuration.proxy_ to configure the _data-plane_ egress](https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/blob/4585fa11d8263470dd3e51d5ccfe3a27ac3e3a4e/api/hypershift/v1beta1/hostedcluster_types.go#L2288-L2295).  However, the cluster-version operator running on the management-cluster control plane has assumed that hosted-cluster Proxy resource should configure its own egress as well.  I’m not sure how folks expect management-cluster-side control-plane egress to be configured, but we don’t want it to respect _spec.configuration.proxy_, because management-cluster networking and data-plane-compute networking are very different environments.

**Version-Release number of selected component**

[The proxy knob has existed since at least 4.14](https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/blame/b11c804b44516f34e50344eeb94901936018d9d1/api/v1beta1/hostedcluster_types.go#L2165-L2167), although [the data-plane docs only date back to 4.18](https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/blame/0d2075c649ed99657f6c5f90a0d273b8f99e9bc2/api/hypershift/v1beta1/hostedcluster_types.go#L1719-L1727).

**How reproducible**

Every time.

**Steps to Reproduce**

1. Create a HostedCluster with spec.configuration.proxy populated with a broken configuration.
2. Check the ClusterVersionRetrievedUpdates condition on the HostedCluster. 

**Actual results**

A message about the broken proxy configuration, like:

```
Unable to retrieve available updates: Get "https://api.openshift.com/api/upgrades_info/v1/graph?...": proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp: lookup ... on ...:53: no such host
```

**Expected results**

Successful Update Service retrieval.

**Additional info**

I’m not clear on how HyperShift clusters are expected to configure proxy egress for management-cluster-side control-plane components.  We will probably need a knob for that eventually.  Maybe we already have a knob, and I just haven’t found it yet?  But in the short term, we can probably just disable CVO respect for the hosted-cluster’s Proxy configuration when the CVO knows it’s running in HyperShift-mode.

summary: Kube 1.35 client-go watcher/informer should detect changes with low latency
description:
**Description of problem:**

The [k8s.io/client-go](http://k8s.io/client-go) vendor bump in [cvo#1282](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/pull/1282) is causing the CVO’s watch of the version-… Pod to go from “notices pretty immediately after the Pod exits” to “takes 4 minutes to notice”.  That’s a significant latency bump, and we shold fix whatever’s changed in the client to get latency back down.

**Version-Release number of selected component**

Seen in the 4.22 CVO when bumping to Kube 1.35 client-go libraries.

**How reproducible:**

Every time.

**Steps to Reproduce:**

Run update CI with the new clients in the source-version CVO.

**Actual results:**

Takes around 4m for the CVO to ack the update request.

**Expected results:**

Reliably than 2m to ack the update request.

**Additional info:**

Pre-update CVO logs like:  

```
  I0320 11:17:58.306414       1 reflector.go:1159] "Warning: event bookmark expired" err="k8s.io/client-go/tools/watch/informerwatcher.go:162: hasn't received required bookmark event marking the end of initial events stream, received last event 18.795159461s ago"
  I0320 11:18:08.306345       1 reflector.go:1159] "Warning: event bookmark expired" err="k8s.io/client-go/tools/watch/informerwatcher.go:162: hasn't received required bookmark event marking the end of initial events stream, received last event 28.795076029s ago"
  ...
  I0320 11:21:18.307818       1 reflector.go:1159] "Warning: event bookmark expired" err="k8s.io/client-go/tools/watch/informerwatcher.go:162: hasn't received required bookmark event marking the end of initial events stream, received last event 3m38.796537922s ago"
  
```

After fixing, revert <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/openshift/origin/pull/30917</custom>

summary: Using BGP RouteAdvertisements with UDN does not add route from target vrf route table to the UDN vrf route table
description:
**Description of problem: If I create a seondary NIC with a VRF and target that VRF with a CUDN and RouteAdvertisments the route from the secondary NIC cannot route incoming traffic into the CUDN VRF as the route is not added to the Target VRF.**

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

**How reproducible:**

**Steps to Reproduce:**  
1\. Configure a secondary NIC  
2\. Attach secondary NIC to a VRF  
3\.  Create a Primary CUDN  
4\. Create a RouteAdvertisement for the CUDN with a targetvrf to step 2

**Actual results: Traffic cannot get to the CUDN vrf route table, if you ping from within a vm/pod on the cudn the traffic reaches the target host as validated with TCPdump and the reply goes back into the host secondary nic in step 1 and route table in step 2.  The route table in step 2 then routes the reply back out to the default route on that vrf route table and creates a loop of traffic.**

**Expected results:**

The route table from step 2 should have a dynamicly created route to route the CUDN traffic from VRF route table in step 2 to the VRF route table interface of the defined subnets in step 3.

**Additional info:**

If I manually define a route subnet prefix defined in step 3 to the route table created as the outcome from step 2 inbound and egress traffic routes correctly to the layer 2 cudn pods/vms.

summary: OCP MERGE FDP-1557: OVN routers cause exact match on IPv6 external addresses in OVS datapath
description:
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-76403#icft=OCPBUGS-76403</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-76277#icft=OCPBUGS-76277</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-74492#icft=OCPBUGS-74492</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-3">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-66428#icft=OCPBUGS-66428</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-4">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-65909#icft=OCPBUGS-65909</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-5">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-63577#icft=OCPBUGS-63577</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-6">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-60781#icft=OCPBUGS-60781</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—

###  Problem Description:

When a packet goes from a container through OVN router to an external IP through a localnet port, we end up with a datapath flow in OVS with a very narrow match (in most cases - exact match) on IPv6 destination address.  If we have a lot clients making requests from a large number of IPv6 addresses external to OVN setup, this is causing creation of one datapath flow per client IP and overwhelming the node.

The reason for this exact-match is a match on ip6.mcast_rsvd addresses that uses non-contiguous match on 124 bits of the IPv6 address that make prefix tracking optimization enabled in <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-7">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/FDP-1024#icft=FDP-1024</custom> ineffective for the ipv6_dst field.

###  Impact Assessment:

If we have more than 200K external IPv6 clients of an OCP service, we may hit the datapath flow limit resulting in upcall storm on the node, reduced performance and potential packet drops.

###  Software Versions:

Reported on OCP 4.14.53 with OVN 24.03.5-40.el9fdp and OVS 3.3.4-110.el9fdp.

###   Issue Type:

Always broken. 

###  Reproducibility:

100%

###  Reproduction Steps:

1\. Create a setup with a tcp server behind LSP connected through an OVN router to a different logical switch with a localnet port.  
2\. Send requests from multiple external IPv6 addresses to that LSP.  
3\. Check ovs-appctl dpctl/dump-flows and observe that there is a separate datapath flow for lsp-to-external direction per external

summary: Broken statefulset rollouts require manual intervention to be fixed
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
Starting with Kubernetes 1.35 (e.g. OCP 4.22), statefulsets managed by the Prometheus operator would require manual intervention when the user tries to revert a configuration which resulted in a stuck rollout of the pods (for instance, node selector matching no nodes).
Previously the statefulset controller would recreate the pods in error.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.22
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Edit the CMO configmap and add `nodeSelector: {foo: bar}` under the prometheusK8s entry.
2. Wait the monitoring ClusterOperator's status reports Degraded=True (due to retries it takes more than 15 minutes to reach this state).
3. Revert the change to the CMO configmap.
```

**Actual results:**

```
The monitoring ClusterOperator's status reports Degraded=True.
```

**Expected results:**

```
The monitoring ClusterOperator's status stays Degraded=False indefinitely.
```

**Additional info:**

```
See https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/pull/8443 and https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/issues/8205 for the details of the issue.
The workaround as of now is to delete the stuck pod.
```

summary: OCP4.18 - Upgrade step-through from 4.14 to 4.18 each time resulted in degraded node state caused by stalled resolv-prepender (wait-for-br-ex-up) requiring manual correction
description:
**Description of problem:**

During a migration step-through process from 4.14 to 4.18 at each upgrade version master nodes would not return to ready until the service wait-for-br-ex-up was manually stopped, which allowed continued boot to kubelet and crio READY states. 

After a review of the journal on the nodes, it was revealed that the service never completed because we never successfully reached the resolv-prepend exit condition that would create the required file. As a result, the node stalled until we bypassed the sleep condition.

From what I can tell in the journal, there is evidence that the br-ex interface did come up, DHCP was applied successfully, nameservers were defined properly and `/etc/resolv.conf`was populated as expected. However, the conditional required resolv-prepend task failed to complete in 30s regardless, leading to exit check state.

We do not appear to have any re-try or re-validate logic on that task after network change is finished. We see multiple attempts to fire the job which seem to succeed, but the one we need to succeed (which is the one fired when br-ex comes up) does not. 

```
SEE FIRST COMMENT BELOW FOR LOG REVIEW
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

**How reproducible:**

Every time - one environment, - not replicated externally yet. 

**Steps to Reproduce:**  
1.  
2.  
3\. 

**Actual results:**

Platform node upgrades are stalled (and I would also expect restarts would be conditionally blocked - but this hasn’t been tested)

**Expected results:**

Node reboot + DHCPv4 allocation should not stall, resolv-prepend should finish and our conditional check for br-ex up should succeed 

**Additional info:**

Primary case linked to bug: 04368605

Case Sosreports and previous conditional blockers and workarounds in case: 04357886

Adding KCS: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7140045</custom>  Which describes the problem + workaround , which appears to be:

Create (Via MC) an updated version of the file: `/etc/NetworkManager/dispatcher.d/30-resolv-prepender` which is hosted here: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/blob/release-4.14/templates/common/on-prem/files/NetworkManager-resolv-prepender.yaml</custom>   which allows the boot to succeed. Existing version of this file is NOT updated on upgrade/manifest, and is assumed to be correctly set because new installs (4.14 and later) place this new version of the config. Previous cluster builds (4.13 and earlier) do not include this data.

summary: ICSP entries with trailing "/" in source are silently ignored by MCO
description:
**Description of problem:**

When configuring `ImageContentSourcePolicy`, entries with a trailing slash (`/`) in the `source` field are rejected by the MachineConfigOperator and not applied to nodes.

Example of failing config:

```
repositoryDigestMirrors:
- source: my.fqdn/
```

Observed behavior:

* MCO does not apply the ICSP
* No clear validation error surfaced to user
* Nodes do not receive updated registry mirror configuration
* Leads to downstream failures such as:

    * Operator bundle unpack failures (DeadlineExceeded)
    * Image pull failures
    

Working configurations:

```
- source: my.fqdn
- source: my.fqdn/foo
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (RHOCP)
- 4
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Yes
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Create an ImageContentSourcePolicy (ICSP) with a trailing `/` in the `source` field:

    ```
    apiVersion: operator.openshift.io/v1alpha1
    kind: ImageContentSourcePolicy
    metadata:
      name: test-icsp
    spec:
      repositoryDigestMirrors:
      - source: my.fqdn/
        mirrors:
        - mirror.fqdn
    ```
2. Apply the ICSP:

    ```
    oc apply -f icsp.yaml
    ```
3. Observe the status of the **Machine Config Operator**:

    ```
    oc get mcp
    ```
4. Verify that the ICSP is not reflected on the nodes (e.g., check `/etc/containers/registries.conf` via debug).
5. Attempt to install an operator (e.g., **OADP Operator**) in a disconnected/mirrored environment.
6. Check Subscription status:

    ```
    oc get sub -n <namespace> -o yaml
    ```

 

**Actual results:**

* ICSP entries with a trailing `/` in the `source` field are silently ignored and not applied to nodes.
* No explicit validation error is shown to the user.
* The MachineConfigPool may appear updated, but registry mirror configuration is not propagated correctly.
* Operator installation fails with:

    * `BundleUnpackFailed`
    * `DeadlineExceeded` during bundle unpack job
    
* No CSV, pods, or related resources are created for the operator.

**Expected results:**

* ICSP entries with trailing `/` should either:

    * Be normalized and applied correctly, **or**
    * Be rejected with a clear validation error during creation
    
* Registry mirror configuration should be consistently applied to all nodes.
* Operator installations via OLM should succeed without bundle unpack failures when ICSP is correctly configured.

**Additional info:**

* Working ICSP configurations:

    ```
    - source: my.fqdn
    - source: my.fqdn/foo
    ```
* Impact:

    * Breaks operator installation in disconnected or mirrored environments
    * Leads to silent failures and difficult troubleshooting

must-gather: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://drive.google.com/file/d/1P4iVVVlD_JlvdpqMdLSAlmAX2cEwYdUQ/view?usp=sharing</custom> 

‌

One issue is that we are seeing stale mpX bindings in ovn-controller:

```
2026-03-12T21:31:53.574633905Z 2026-03-12T21:31:53.574Z|34567|binding|WARN|Invalid configuration: iface-id is configured on interfaces : [ovn-k8s-mp1258] and [ovn-k8s-mp748]. Ignoring the configuration on interface [ovn-k8s-mp748]
```

This doesn’t immediately explain the issue with the service access but is certainly a bug we need to fix. 

I think this might be caused by the fact that we are not cleaning up if startup failed, requires more investigation.

summary: User from namespace B can access the private image from namespace A
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
When enabling the feature gate "KubeletEnsureSecretPulledImages", user from namespace B can access the private image from namespace A
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-03-13-184504
```

**How reproducible:**

```
always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Enable featuregate : KubeletEnsureSecretPulledImages (The default CredentialsVerificationPolicy is "NeverVerifyPreloadedImages")
2. create namespace tenant-a 
3. create ImagePullSecret secret-a for image quay.io/minmli/crio-credential-private:latest in namespace tenant-a
% oc create secret docker-registry minmli-quay-secret --docker-server=quay.io --docker-username=minmli --docker-password=XXXX
4. in namespace tenant-a, create a pod pulling above image with IfNotPresent ImagePullPolicy and specify one node in spec.nodeName
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    app: test
  name: test-secret-a1
spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  restartPolicy: Always
  imagePullSecrets:
  - name: minmli-quay-secret
  nodeName: ci-ln-hw53gmk-72292-v49xs-worker-a-52phq  
  containers:
  - name: test
    command: ['sh', '-c', 'echo "Hello, world!" && sleep 3600']
    image: quay.io/minmli/crio-credential-private:latest
    imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
5. create namespace tenant-b
6. in namespace tenant-b, create a pod pulling the same image and specify the same node but without ImagePullSecret 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    app: test
  name: test-secret-b1
spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  restartPolicy: Always
  nodeName: ci-ln-hw53gmk-72292-v49xs-worker-a-52phq  
  containers:
  - name: test
    command: ['sh', '-c', 'echo "Hello, world!" && sleep 3600']
    image: quay.io/minmli/crio-credential-private:latest
    imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
```

**Actual results:**

```
4.the image was pulled successfully and the pod started
6.the pod started and the Events show: Container image "quay.io/minmli/crio-credential-private:latest" already present on machine and can be accessed by the pod
```

**Expected results:**

```
6. the pod created failed, it should prompt credential verification failed
```

**Additional info:**

```
I also try creating a kubeletconfig to specify the CredentialsVerificationPolicy as NeverVerifyPreloadedImages, the kubeletConfig is applied but the policy didn't take effect either.
apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
kind: KubeletConfig
metadata:
  name: credential-verification-policy
spec:
  machineConfigPoolSelector:
    matchLabels:
      pools.operator.machineconfiguration.openshift.io/worker: ""
  kubeletConfig:
    ima

summary: prometheus-operator-admission-webhook does not honor APIServer tlsSecurityProfile
description:
**Description of problem:**

tested with cluster-bot

```
launch 4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-03-15-203841,openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator#2841 aws
```

edit APIServer to update tlsSecurityProfile to use VersionTLS11 cipher suite

```
$ oc get APIServer cluster -oyaml
...
spec:
  audit:
    profile: Default
  tlsSecurityProfile:
    custom:
      ciphers:
      - ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
      - ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
      - ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384
      - ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384
      - ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305
      minTLSVersion: VersionTLS11
    type: Custom
```

tls-cipher-suites and tls-min-version for kubeapiserver is also updated

```
$ oc get kubeapiservers cluster -o json | jq .spec.observedConfig.servingInfo
{
  "bindAddress": "0.0.0.0:6443",
  "bindNetwork": "tcp4",
  "cipherSuites": [
    "TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256",
    "TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256",

summary: 4.20- Custom serviceAccountIssuer unsets service-account-jwks-uri
description:
Description of problem:

```java
When a custom serviceAccountIssuer is set on the Authentication CR, the service-account-jwks-uri argument in the KubeAPIServer CR will no longer be set to https://<API_URL>/openid/v1/jwks. 

This means that the jwks_uri field from the /.well-known/openid-configurations endpoint will now be set to https://<MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443/openid/v1/jwks. The problem is that even if the client machine is configured to trust the master node's certificate CA, there will still be an error because the certificate's SAN does not include the master node's IP.
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
4.17.6
```

How reproducible:

```java
This is a reproducible issue
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```java
1. Patch the authentication CR with a custom serviceAccountIssuer.
~~~
oc patch authentication cluster --type merge --patch '{"spec": {"serviceAccountIssuer": "https://test.com"}}'
~~~

2. Wait for any rollouts to complete as seen with oc get co. If any pods go into CrashLoopBackOff then delete them with this command:
~~~
oc get pods --all-namespaces | grep CrashLoopBackOff | awk '{print "--namespace " $1 " " $2}' | xargs -L 1 oc delete pod
~~~

3. Check the value of jwks_uri from the well-known endpoint:
~~~
oc get --raw /.well-known/openid-configuration | jq -r '.jwks_uri'
https://<MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443/openid/v1/jwks
~~~     

4. Trust the master node certificate CA which appears to be kube-apiserver-service-network-signer by default.
~~~
oc extract secret/service-network-serving-signer -n openshift-kube-apiserver-operator

sudo cp tls.crt /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/tls.crt
sudo update-ca-trust
~~~

5. Curl the master node IP.
~~~
curl https://<MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443
curl: (60) SSL: no alternative certificate subject name matches target host name '<MASTER_NODE_IP>'
~~~

6. Check the SAN of the master node's certificate.
~~~
openssl s_client -connect <MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443 -showcerts 2>&1 | openssl x509 -text | grep 'Subject Alternative Name' -A 1
            X509v3 Subject Alternative Name: 
                DNS:kubernetes, DNS:kubernetes.default, DNS:kubernetes.default.svc, DNS:kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local, DNS:openshift, DNS:openshift.default, DNS:openshift.default.svc, DNS:openshift.default.svc.cluster.local, DNS:172.30.0.1, IP Address:172.30.0.1
~~~
```

Actual results:

```java
The value of jwks_uri is not accessible with TLS.
```

Expected results:

```java

summary: Document composefs implications for RHCOS 4.19
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
With RHCOS 4.19, composefs has been introduced.
More information here: https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora-coreos/composefs/

From the above Fedora documentation:
"The main visible change will be that the root filesystem (/) is now small and full (a few MB, 100% used)."
... this impacts end users with disk monitoring on their RHCOS nodes to be alerted that root disk usage is at 100%.

Requesting amendments to OpenShift/RHCOS 4.19 documentation to highlight the above change, impact, and recommendation to adjust node monitoring for this specific scenario.

summary: Ignition server may fail to set ignition-reached annotation on token secret due to unretried conflict
description:
Description of problem:

```
The ignition server HTTP handler sets the ignition-reached=True annotation on the
token secret after serving the ignition payload to a node. This annotation is how
the NodePool controller determines that the ReachedIgnitionEndpoint condition should
be True.

The Get+Update used to set this annotation has no retry-on-conflict logic. The
TokenSecretReconciler, running in the same process, may concurrently patch the same
token secret with payload data, which could bump the resourceVersion and cause the
handler's Update to fail with a 409 Conflict. This error is silently logged with no
retry, potentially losing the annotation.

InPlace upgrade NodePools are more susceptible because:
- The TokenSecretReconciler writes compressed payload data into the token secret
- Nodes contact the ignition endpoint exactly once during bootstrap (no reboots)
- Replace/Rolling nodes contact the endpoint multiple times, providing additional
  opportunities for the annotation to succeed
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```
Affects 4.18, 4.19, 4.20, 4.21, and main. The code is identical on all branches.
The vulnerable code path has existed since commit 435f6de4f4 (Dec 2022).
```

How reproducible:

```
Intermittent. Depends on timing between the HTTP handler's Update and the
TokenSecretReconciler's Patch on the same token secret. More likely for InPlace
upgrade NodePools.
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```
1. Create a HostedCluster with a NodePool using upgradeType: InPlace
2. Wait for the node to bootstrap and contact the ignition server
3. Observe the ReachedIgnitionEndpoint condition on the NodePool
```

Actual results:

```
The ReachedIgnitionEndpoint condition may remain False with reason
ignitionNotReached indefinitely, despite the node having successfully fetched
its ignition payload (confirmed by GetPayload event on the token secret). The
NodePool never reaches a fully healthy status and tests like
TestNodePoolInPlaceUpgrade time out.
```

Expected results:

```
The ReachedIgnitionEndpoint condition transitions to True after the node
successfully contacts the ignition server endpoint.
```

Additional info:

```
Evidence from CI run (build 2036678754327072768):
- Token secret events show 65x GetPayloadFailed followed by 1x GetPayload at
  07:14:15 (payload was served successfully)
- ReachedIgnitionEndpoint stayed False from 07:07:41 and never transitioned
- All other NodePools (Replace type) on the same HostedCluster got
  ReachedIgnitionEndpoint=True
- Ignition server logs were scrubbed from artifacts, so the actual 409 error
  could not be directly observed

Fix: https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/pull/8066
Wraps the Get+Update in retry.RetryOnConflict to handle concurrent modifications.
```

summary: [release-4.20] parseIPList logs "empty list found" at V(0) causing excessive log spam
description:
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-79374#icft=OCPBUGS-79374</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
**Description of problem:**

The \`parseIPList\` function in \`pkg/router/template/template_helper.go\` logs at verbosity level \`V(0)\` when it receives an empty IP allowlist string. Since \`V(0)\` messages are always emitted regardless of log verbosity settings, this causes massive log spam on clusters with many routes that don't have \`haproxy.router.openshift.io/ip_allowlist\` annotations.

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```java
N/A
```

How reproducible: **Always**

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```java
1. Deploy OKD/OpenShift 4.x cluster
    2. Create multiple routes without `ip_allowlist` or `ip_whitelist` annotations
    3. Observe router pod logs: `oc logs -n openshift-ingress -l ingresscontroller.operator.openshift.io/deployment-ingresscontroller=default`
    4. Trigger a config reload (create/modify any route, service, or endpoint)
    5. Observe flood of "parseIPList empty list found" messages
```

**Actual results:**

On a cluster with 822 routes (none with allowlist annotations), every config reload generates \***822 log messages**\*:

```java
I0108 15:56:42.194350  1 template_helper.go:370] "msg"="parseIPList empty list found" "logger"="template"
I0108 15:56:42.194433  1 template_helper.go:370] "msg"="parseIPList empty list found" "logger"="template"
I0108 15:56:42.194511  1 template_helper.go:370] "msg"="parseIPList empty list found" "logger"="template"
... (repeated 822 times per reload)
```

**Expected results:**

An empty/absent IP allowlist is the **normal, expected configuration** for most routes. This should not generate a log message at V(0). The log level should be V(7) or higher (debug/trace level) to match other informational messages in the same function.

**Additional info:**

Also filed github issue [https://github.com/openshift/router/issues/708](https://github.com/openshift/router/issues/708).

summary: HyperShift CPO validateAzureKMSConfig permanently fails with 401 Unauthorized for Self-Managed Azure clusters
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
 For Self-Managed Azure clusters with KMS encryption enabled, the credential object remains `nil`. The CPO then uses this `nil` credential to make an `Encrypt` API call to the Azure Key Vault, resulting in
an unauthenticated request. 

This causes the Key Vault to reject the request with a `401 Unauthorized` error:
AKV10000: Request is missing a Bearer, PoP, or MTLS_POP token.
Error info:
    eventually.go:228:  - incorrect condition: wanted ClusterVersionProgressing=False, got ClusterVersionProgressing=Unknown: StatusUnknown(Condition not found in the CVO.)

summary: Worker nodes using GCP machine type 'n1-standard-1' with GPU enabled fail to join cluster
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
GPU-enabled worker nodes using GCP machine type n1-standard-1 (1 vCPU, 3.75GB RAM) fail to join the OpenShift cluster in nightly version 4.20/4.21/4.22, while the same configuration works correctly in version 4.19.

The GCP instance is created successfully and reaches RUNNING state, but the node never registers with the cluster. The machine remains stuck in "Provisioned" phase indefinitely, with machine controller continuously logging "has no node yet, requeuing" every 30 seconds.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
**Failing Version (OpenShift 4.20)**:
- OpenShift: 4.20.0-0.ci-2026-03-25-021633
- Kubernetes: v1.33.9
- CRI-O: 1.33.10-2.rhaos4.20.gita4d0894.el9
- RHCOS: Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS 9.6.20260323-1 (Plow)
- Kernel: 5.14.0-570.102.1.el9_6.x86_64

**Working Version (OpenShift 4.19)**:
- OpenShift: 4.19.0-0.ci-2026-03-25-021733
- Kubernetes: v1.32.13
- CRI-O: 1.32.13-5.rhaos4.19.gitcd42990.el9
- RHCOS: Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS 9.6.20260323-1 (Plow)
- Kernel: 5.14.0-570.102.1.el9_6.x86_64

**Key Differences**:
- Kubernetes: 1.32.13 → 1.33.9
- CRI-O: 1.32.13 → 1.33.10
- RHCOS and Kernel: Identical
```

**How reproducible:**

```
100% on OpenShift 4.20, 4.21, 4.22 nightly clusters with the specified configuration.
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

````
1. Deploy fresh 4.20 nightly cluster on GCP (standard IPI installation)

2. Verify cluster is healthy with existing worker nodes running

3. Create a machineset with GPU configuration
```yaml
apiVersion: machine.openshift.io/v1beta1
kind: MachineSet
metadata:
  name: gpu-test-420
  namespace: openshift-machine-api
spec:
  replicas: 1
  template:
    spec:
      providerSpec:
        value:
          apiVersion: machine.openshift.io/v1beta1
          kind: GCPMachineProviderSpec
          machineType: n1-standard-1
          zone: us-central1-c
          region: us-central1
          gpus:
            - count: 1
              type: nvidia-tesla-p100
          onHostMaintenance: Terminate
          restartPolicy: Always
          disks:
            - autoDelete: true
              boot: true
              sizeGb: 128
              type: pd-balanced
```

4. Apply the machineset: `oc create -f machineset.yaml`

5. Monitor the machine status:
```bash
oc get machine -n openshift-machine-api | grep gpu-test-420
```

6. Wait for 60+ minutes and observe that machine never transitions to "Running" phase

7. Check for node:
```bash
oc get nodes | grep gpu-test-420
```
````

**Actual results:**

````
### Machine Status (stuck in Provisioned)
```bash
$ oc get machine -n openshift-machine-api | grep gpu-test-420
gpu-test-420-kkx6m   Provisioned   n1-standard-1   us-central1   us-central1-c   69m
```

### Machine Details
```yaml
status:
  phase: Provisioned  # Never changes to "Running"
  addresses:
    - address: 10.0.128.6
      type: InternalIP
    - address: gpu-test-420-kkx6m.us-central1-c.c.openshift-gce-devel-ci.internal

description:
OpenShift Container Platform 4.12  notice end of Extended update support

This is an automated  notification for the end of Extended update support for OpenShift Container Platform 4.12 on January 17, 2025.  
This notification applies only to those customers operating OpenShift Container Platform 4.12.  
As part of the Extended update support, qualified security patches of critical or important impact, as well as select mission-critical bug-fix patches, were released for OpenShift Container Platform 4.12. 

On January 17, 2025, OpenShift Container Platform 4.12 transitions into its Extended Update Support (EUS) phase, updates may be available for Critical and Important security advisories as well as Urgent priority bugs.  Customers should check for any additional subscription requirements in order to consume these updates, during the EUS phase. Please consult with your sales team to understand more about Extended Update offerings. 

At the end of Extended update support, software and documentation will continue to be available to customers throughout the time OpenShift Container Platform 4 remains available. 

Please see the OpenShift Container Platform 4 life cycle for further information about OpenShift Container Platform 4 releases and life cycle phases: [https://access.redhat.com/support/policy/updates/openshift/](https://access.redhat.com/support/policy/updates/openshift/)

description:
OpenShift Container Platform 4.18 four month notice end of Maintenance support

This is an automated four month notification for the end of Maintenance support for OpenShift Container Platform 4.18 on August 25, 2026.  
This notification applies only to those customers operating OpenShift Container Platform 4.18.  
As part of the Maintenance support, qualified security patches of critical or important impact, as well as select mission-critical bug-fix patches, were released for OpenShift Container Platform 4.18. 

On August 25, 2026, OpenShift Container Platform 4.18 transitions into its Extended Update Support (EUS) phase, updates may be available for Critical and Important security advisories as well as Urgent priority bugs.  Customers should check for any additional subscription requirements in order to consume these updates, during the EUS phase. Please consult with your sales team to understand more about Extended Update offerings. 

At the end of Maintenance support, software and documentation will continue to be available to customers throughout the time OpenShift Container Platform 4 remains available. 

Please see the OpenShift Container Platform 4 life cycle for further information about OpenShift Container Platform 4 releases and life cycle phases: [https://access.redhat.com/support/policy/updates/openshift/](https://access.redhat.com/support/policy/updates/openshift/)

For important instructions on how to install or upgrade to the latest version of OpenShift Container Platform, see the following documentation: [https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.18/updating/updating-cluster-within-minor.html](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.18/updating/updating-cluster-within-minor.html)

summary: driver-toolkit v4.21.7 container image switch of microdnf breaks builds that rely on "dnf install $REMOTE_RPM_URL"
description:
With the release of driver-toolkit v4.21.7, it migrated from `dnf `→ `microdnf` for package management.  This broke a container build workflow that a partner relied on to download and install the EPEL repo rpm from a remote url due to a limitation in \`microdnf\` v3.9 not supporting standalone installation of RPMs (remote or local).    

```shell
# This works in DTK 4.21.6 but breaks in 4.21.7
$ wget https://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/epel-release-latest-9.noarch.rpm && dnf install -y epel-release-latest-9.noarch.rpm
$ dnf config-manager --add-repo=https://mirror.stream.centos.org/9-stream/AppStream/x86_64/os/
```

  
I don’t feel this qualifies as an actual bug but it feels like something we should be aware of for additional workflows that could be affected.

summary: NMStateConfig discarded as "dirty" when agent-config is on disk
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
When using the agent-based installer in the OVE (OpenShift Virtualization Extension) workflow,providing both `agent-config.yaml` (with only rendezvousIP) and `nmstateconfig.yaml` (with network configuration) results in the nmstateconfig.yaml being discarded and the network configuration being lost in the generated unconfigured ignition file.  This occurs because the asset store's dependency tracking incorrectly treats user-provided configuration files as "dirty dependencies" that require regeneration of child assets, even when both files are independently user-provided and should coexist.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Create agent-config.yaml with only rendezvousIP (no hosts section)
2. Create `cluster-manifests` directory and add nmstateconfig.yaml, infraenv.yaml, pull-secret.yaml
3. Run the command `./openshift-install agent create unconfigured-ignition --log-level debug`
```

**Actual results:**

```
1. The installer outputs a warning:  WARNING: Discarding the NMState Config that was provided in the target directory  because its dependencies are dirty and it needs to be regenerated  
2. The NMState Config asset is regenerated, but since agent-config.yaml has no hosts  defined (only rendezvousIP), the regeneration produces an empty NMStateConfig.  
3. The generated unconfigured-agent.ign file does NOT contain the network configuration  files:    - Missing: /etc/assisted/network/host0/eth0.nmconnection    - Missing: /etc/assisted/network/host1/eth0.nmconnection    - Missing: /etc/assisted/network/host2/eth0.nmconnection    - Missing: /etc/assisted/network/*/mac_interface.ini  
4. The user-provided network configuration from nmstateconfig.yaml is completely lost.  
5. Debug log shows:  DEBUG Discarding the NMState Config that was provided in the target directory because  its dependencies are dirty and it needs to be regenerated  DEBUG Generating NMState Config...  DEBUG   (exits early because agentHosts.Hosts is empty)
```

**Expected results:**

```
1. No warning should be displayed about discarding the NMState Config.  
2. Both user-provided configuration files should be preserved:    - agent-config.yaml provides the rendezvousIP    - nmstateconfig.yaml provides the network configuration  
3. The generated unconfigured-agent.ign file SHOULD contain all network configuration  files from nmstateconfig.yaml:    - /etc/assisted/network/host0/eth0.nmconnection    - /etc/assisted/network/host1/eth0.nmconnection    - /etc/assisted/network/host2/eth0.nmconnection    - /etc/assisted/network/*/mac_interface.ini    - /usr/local/bin/pre-network-manager-config.sh  
4. The pre-network-manager-config.service should be enabled in the ignition.  
5. Debug log should show:  DEBUG Using NMState Config loaded from target directory (preserving user-provided  config despite on-disk dependencies)  DEBUG Using Age

summary: OVE local UI shows "cluster details not found" error and blocks cluster creation after booting ISO
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
When booting the OVE ISO, the TUI displays the browser URL message ("Please go to http://rendezvousIP:3001 in your browser to continue the installation") before the agent-register-infraenv systemd service completes. This creates a race condition where users can access the local UI before the infrastructure environment is properly registered.

```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. ```
  Create OVE ISO in staging SaaS environment using release 4.21.7
  Fill in all optional configurations in SaaS UI
  Download the generated OVE ISO
  Boot a VM using the downloaded ISO
  Observe TUI message: "Please go to http://rendezvousIP:3001 in your browser to continue the installation"
  Immediately open browser and navigate to the displayed URL (act within 5-10 seconds)
  Enter cluster name, base domain, and pull secret in the form
  Click "Next" button
  Observe: Error message "cluster details not found"
  Wait 15-20 seconds, then refresh the browser page
  Observe: UI displays "hosts discover" page, skipping virtualization bundle selection
  ```

**Actual results:**

```
  Scenario 1: User acts quickly

User sees the TUI message and immediately opens the browser ( local UI)

Enters cluster name, base domain, and pull secret

Clicks "Next" button

Error displayed: "cluster details not found"

UI does not allow cluster configuration to proceed



Scenario 2: User refreshes after error

User refreshes the browser page

By this time, agent-register-infraenv has completed successfully

UI incorrectly jumps directly to "hosts discover" page

Virtualization bundle selection page is completely skipped

User cannot select virtualization bundles as intended

Breaks the expected OVE deployment workflow
```

**Expected results:**

```
TUI should only display the browser URL message after agent-register-infraenv service completes successfully

Local UI should load with proper infrastructure environment registration

UI should present the complete configuration workflow:
a. Cluster details entry
b. Virtualization bundle selection
c. Hosts discover

User should be able to complete full cluster configuration without errors or skipped steps
```

**Additional info:**

```
Root Cause

The check_ui() function only verifies that the agent-ui service is active before displaying the browser URL message. It does not wait for or validate that agent-register-infraenv has completed successfully. installer/data/data/agent/files/usr/local/bin/install-status.sh at main · openshift/installer  

Environment

OpenShift Virtualization Environment (OVE)
Deployment: SaaS Staging
OCP Release: quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-release:4.21.7-x86_64
Assisted-installer staging UI can be reached at http://10.1.155.137:6008
```

summary: CIO runs at DEBUG log level by default, causing excessive log volume with Sail Library integration
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
The cluster-ingress-operator uses zap.NewDevelopment() which sets the default log level to DEBUG. This causes excessive log volume, particularly problematic with the Sail Library integration for Gateway API which generates approximately 2,000 debug logs per hour during normal operation.

Debug-level logs from the Sail Library include drift detection events, reconciliation triggers, and resource watching that are not relevant for production use and create significant noise in log aggregation systems.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.22
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Deploy cluster-ingress-operator with Gateway API feature enabled (TechPreview or using Sail Library)
2. Create a GatewayClass using openshift-default controller
3. Observe operator logs in Loki or via `oc logs -n openshift-ingress-operator deployment/ingress-operator`
4. Filter for DEBUG level logs from operator.init.install (Sail Library)
```

**Actual results:**

```
- Operator logs at DEBUG level by default
- Sail Library generates ~2,000 debug logs per hour including:
  - "Skipping update (predicate)" messages
  - "Skipping update (not owned)" messages
  - Resource watching and drift detection events
- Excessive log volume in production environments
```

**Expected results:**

```
- Operator should default to INFO log level for production
- Sail Library debug logs (V(1), V(2)) should not appear at INFO level
```

**Additional info:**

```
Current implementation in pkg/log/log.go uses zap.NewDevelopment() which defaults to DEBUG level with no runtime configuration option.

Suggested fix:
1. Change zap.NewDevelopment() to zap.NewProduction() or configure with InfoLevel as default
2. Audit existing log.V(1).Info() and log.V(2).Info() calls in cluster-ingress-operator codebase - promote to log.Info() if the information is necessary for production troubleshooting

This would reduce log volume while maintaining visibility into important operational events.
```

summary: [assisted-service] Agent-based installer ignores all but first NMStateConfig in multi-document YAML
description:
The `RegisterInfraEnv` function in `cmd/agentbasedinstaller/register.go` only processes the first NMStateConfig document when the input YAML file contains multiple documents separated by `---`. This causes network configuration for subsequent hosts to be silently ignored during agent-based installations.

‌

## Expected Behavior

   Both NMStateConfig documents should be parsed and added to the InfraEnv's StaticNetworkConfig array. The log should show:

```
Added 2 nmstateconfigs
```

## Actual Behavior

   Only the first NMStateConfig document is processed. The second one is silently ignored. The log shows:

```
Added 1 nmstateconfigs
```

## Root Cause

   **File:** `cmd/agentbasedinstaller/register.go:220-224` <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/openshift/assisted-service/blob/c313528fddb88cb401226ac1fdb149aff06b9461/cmd/agentbasedinstaller/register.go#L220-L224</custom> 

   The code uses `getFileData()` which calls `yaml.Unmarshal()`. This function only unmarshals the first YAML document in a multi-document stream:

```go
// Line 220 - only reads first document
if nmStateErr := getFileData(nmStateConfigPath, &nmStateConfig); nmStateErr != nil {
    return nil, nmStateErr
}
```

   From `sigs.k8s.io/yaml` documentation:

> "Multi-document unmarshalling is not yet implemented"

## Impact

* Users with multiple hosts requiring static network configuration must work around by:

    * Creating separate files for each NMStateConfig (inefficient)
    * Using external tooling to split multi-document YAML
    
* Silent failure makes this difficult to debug
* Standard Kubernetes practice is to support multi-document YAML (kubectl apply supports it)

summary: [release-4.21]NMStateConfig discarded as "dirty" when agent-config is on disk
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
When using the agent-based installer in the OVE (OpenShift Virtualization Extension) workflow,providing both `agent-config.yaml` (with only rendezvousIP) and `nmstateconfig.yaml` (with network configuration) results in the nmstateconfig.yaml being discarded and the network configuration being lost in the generated unconfigured ignition file.  This occurs because the asset store's dependency tracking incorrectly treats user-provided configuration files as "dirty dependencies" that require regeneration of child assets, even when both files are independently user-provided and should coexist.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Create agent-config.yaml with only rendezvousIP (no hosts section)
2. Create `cluster-manifests` directory and add nmstateconfig.yaml, infraenv.yaml, pull-secret.yaml
3. Run the command `./openshift-install agent create unconfigured-ignition --log-level debug`
```

**Actual results:**

```
1. The installer outputs a warning:  WARNING: Discarding the NMState Config that was provided in the target directory  because its dependencies are dirty and it needs to be regenerated  
2. The NMState Config asset is regenerated, but since agent-config.yaml has no hosts  defined (only rendezvousIP), the regeneration produces an empty NMStateConfig.  
3. The generated unconfigured-agent.ign file does NOT contain the network configuration  files:    - Missing: /etc/assisted/network/host0/eth0.nmconnection    - Missing: /etc/assisted/network/host1/eth0.nmconnection    - Missing: /etc/assisted/network/host2/eth0.nmconnection    - Missing: /etc/assisted/network/*/mac_interface.ini  
4. The user-provided network configuration from nmstateconfig.yaml is completely lost.  
5. Debug log shows:  DEBUG Discarding the NMState Config that was provided in the target directory because  its dependencies are dirty and it needs to be regenerated  DEBUG Generating NMState Config...  DEBUG   (exits early because agentHosts.Hosts is empty)
```

**Expected results:**

```
1. No warning should be displayed about discarding the NMState Config.  
2. Both user-provided configuration files should be preserved:    - agent-config.yaml provides the rendezvousIP    - nmstateconfig.yaml provides the network configuration  
3. The generated unconfigured-agent.ign file SHOULD contain all network configuration  files from nmstateconfig.yaml:    - /etc/assisted/network/host0/eth0.nmconnection    - /etc/assisted/network/host1/eth0.nmconnection    - /etc/assisted/network/host2/eth0.nmconnection    - /etc/assisted/network/*/mac_interface.ini    - /usr/local/bin/pre-network-manager-config.sh  
4. The pre-network-manager-config.service should be enabled in the ignition.  
5. Debug log should show:  DEBUG Using NMState Config loaded from target directory (preserving user-provided  config despite on-disk dependencies)  DEBUG Using Age

summary: [release-4.21]OVE local UI shows "cluster details not found" error and blocks cluster creation after booting ISO
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
When booting the OVE ISO, the TUI displays the browser URL message ("Please go to http://rendezvousIP:3001 in your browser to continue the installation") before the agent-register-infraenv systemd service completes. This creates a race condition where users can access the local UI before the infrastructure environment is properly registered.

```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. ```
  Create OVE ISO in staging SaaS environment using release 4.21.7
  Fill in all optional configurations in SaaS UI
  Download the generated OVE ISO
  Boot a VM using the downloaded ISO
  Observe TUI message: "Please go to http://rendezvousIP:3001 in your browser to continue the installation"
  Immediately open browser and navigate to the displayed URL (act within 5-10 seconds)
  Enter cluster name, base domain, and pull secret in the form
  Click "Next" button
  Observe: Error message "cluster details not found"
  Wait 15-20 seconds, then refresh the browser page
  Observe: UI displays "hosts discover" page, skipping virtualization bundle selection
  ```

**Actual results:**

```
  Scenario 1: User acts quickly

User sees the TUI message and immediately opens the browser ( local UI)

Enters cluster name, base domain, and pull secret

Clicks "Next" button

Error displayed: "cluster details not found"

UI does not allow cluster configuration to proceed



Scenario 2: User refreshes after error

User refreshes the browser page

By this time, agent-register-infraenv has completed successfully

UI incorrectly jumps directly to "hosts discover" page

Virtualization bundle selection page is completely skipped

User cannot select virtualization bundles as intended

Breaks the expected OVE deployment workflow
```

**Expected results:**

```
TUI should only display the browser URL message after agent-register-infraenv service completes successfully

Local UI should load with proper infrastructure environment registration

UI should present the complete configuration workflow:
a. Cluster details entry
b. Virtualization bundle selection
c. Hosts discover

User should be able to complete full cluster configuration without errors or skipped steps
```

**Additional info:**

```
Root Cause

The check_ui() function only verifies that the agent-ui service is active before displaying the browser URL message. It does not wait for or validate that agent-register-infraenv has completed successfully. installer/data/data/agent/files/usr/local/bin/install-status.sh at main · openshift/installer  

Environment

OpenShift Virtualization Environment (OVE)
Deployment: SaaS Staging
OCP Release: quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-release:4.21.7-x86_64
Assisted-installer staging UI can be reached at http://10.1.155.137:6008
```

summary: SR-IOV NodeState stuck in InProgress with Drain_Required after cleanup, blocking DPDK tests
description:
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-78767#icft=OCPBUGS-78767</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
**Description of problem:**

```
<Describe theSR-IOV state remains stuck in InProgress with Drain_Required after cleanup, despite node and MCP recovery, preventing test suite progression. problem here>
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.21.6
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Easy to reproduce.
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. 

    ```
    Deploy OCP 4.21.6 with SR-IOV operator
    Run CNF DPDK test suite
    After a test failure, check: oc get sriovnetworknodestates -n openshift-sriov-network-operator
    Observe node stuck in InProgress (Drain_Required)
    Subsequent tests fail due to SR-IOV not converging
    ```

**Actual results:**

```
SriovNetworkNodeState on worker0 remains stuck with:
 - syncStatus=InProgress
 - desired-state=Drain_Required
 - current-state=DrainComplete

SR-IOV operator continues reconciling but does not update or clear the state
Node is Ready and MCP is Updated=True, but SR-IOV does not converge
Subsequent DPDK tests fail during setup with: sync status still in progress
```

**Expected results:**

```
After SR-IOV cleanup/reset, the node state should converge to:
 - syncStatus=Succeeded
 - desired-state=Idle
 - current-state=Idle

SR-IOV operator should clear any stale drain state and complete reconciliation
Subsequent tests should proceed successfully without being blocked
```

**Additional info:**

```
‌
```

description:
OpenShift Container Platform 4.12  notice end of Extended update support

This is an automated  notification for the end of Extended update support for OpenShift Container Platform 4.12 on January 17, 2025.  
This notification applies only to those customers operating OpenShift Container Platform 4.12.  
As part of the Extended update support, qualified security patches of critical or important impact, as well as select mission-critical bug-fix patches, were released for OpenShift Container Platform 4.12. 

On January 17, 2025, OpenShift Container Platform 4.12 transitions into its Extended Update Support (EUS) phase, updates may be available for Critical and Important security advisories as well as Urgent priority bugs.  Customers should check for any additional subscription requirements in order to consume these updates, during the EUS phase. Please consult with your sales team to understand more about Extended Update offerings. 

At the end of Extended update support, software and documentation will continue to be available to customers throughout the time OpenShift Container Platform 4 remains available. 

Please see the OpenShift Container Platform 4 life cycle for further information about OpenShift Container Platform 4 releases and life cycle phases: [https://access.redhat.com/support/policy/updates/openshift/](https://access.redhat.com/support/policy/updates/openshift/)

summary: RendezvousIP should not be inferred in unconfigured ignition
description:
Since <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/openshift/installer/commit/9dc636fd52b84480f3a14698f2b4e437c47c50c6</custom> we automatically calculate the RendezvousIP in the unconfigured-ignition from NMState data when available, just as we do when creating a regular agent ISO.

This is undesirable, because without knowing the roles assigned to the hosts we may select a host that the user doesn’t intend to be a control plane node. We must have the user explicitly choose a RendezvousIP, either in the above-sea-level UI or on the TUI console.

summary: [release-4.21] parseIPList logs "empty list found" at V(0) causing excessive log spam
description:
**Description of problem:**

The \`parseIPList\` function in \`pkg/router/template/template_helper.go\` logs at verbosity level \`V(0)\` when it receives an empty IP allowlist string. Since \`V(0)\` messages are always emitted regardless of log verbosity settings, this causes massive log spam on clusters with many routes that don't have \`haproxy.router.openshift.io/ip_allowlist\` annotations.

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```java
N/A
```

How reproducible: **Always**

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```java
1. Deploy OKD/OpenShift 4.x cluster
    2. Create multiple routes without `ip_allowlist` or `ip_whitelist` annotations
    3. Observe router pod logs: `oc logs -n openshift-ingress -l ingresscontroller.operator.openshift.io/deployment-ingresscontroller=default`
    4. Trigger a config reload (create/modify any route, service, or endpoint)
    5. Observe flood of "parseIPList empty list found" messages
```

**Actual results:**

On a cluster with 822 routes (none with allowlist annotations), every config reload generates \***822 log messages**\*:

```java
I0108 15:56:42.194350  1 template_helper.go:370] "msg"="parseIPList empty list found" "logger"="template"
I0108 15:56:42.194433  1 template_helper.go:370] "msg"="parseIPList empty list found" "logger"="template"
I0108 15:56:42.194511  1 template_helper.go:370] "msg"="parseIPList empty list found" "logger"="template"
... (repeated 822 times per reload)
```

**Expected results:**

An empty/absent IP allowlist is the **normal, expected configuration** for most routes. This should not generate a log message at V(0). The log level should be V(7) or higher (debug/trace level) to match other informational messages in the same function.

**Additional info:**

Also filed github issue [https://github.com/openshift/router/issues/708](https://github.com/openshift/router/issues/708).

summary: Add extra line to the certificate creation to fix ongoing issue when configuring custom ingress certs
description:
**Description of problem:**

```
When configuring the custom Ingress Certificate, the Cluster Ingress Operator
will create a copy in openshift-config-managed with the domain name that can
be used by Authentincation application.

The problem happens when the Certificate is missing extra new line at the end
causing that the collocated PEM file (certs+private key) is malformed and where
certificate end on the same line the private key starts.

This causes an issue where after configuring the custom Ingress certs, the 
authentication application becomes degraded and usually it ends on the 
situation that case is opened for Red Hat Support.

One could say that this can be added to the docs in order to inform the cluster
admin to be aware of this requirement, however, in many cases it ends up upon
Support team to fix the issue for customer.

In other hand, fixing it in the code with simple fix would prevent this and even
cluster admins that do not configure the extra line requirement would not 
encounter that issue.

More info can be found in the KCS article.
https://access.redhat.com/solutions/6984698
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
OpenShift Container Platform 4.21 (in)
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Create a custom Ingress cert with missing extra line at the end.
You can verify it by running:

$ od -c ingress.pem | tail -n 4

0004420   4   L   q   q   r   c   D   R   g   h   G   f   M   0   A   =
0004440   =  \n   -   -   -   -   -   E   N   D       C   E   R   T   I
0004460   F   I   C   A   T   E   -   -   -   -   -
0004473
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. 
2. 
3. 
```

**Actual results:**

```
the authentication will fail
```

**Expected results:**

```
 code fix will prevent that
```

**Additional info:**

```
‌
```

description:

Description of problem:

panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x38 pc=0x1f94bf0]

goroutine 528 [running]:
github.com/operator-framework/operator-lifecycle-manager/pkg/controller/bundle.sortUnpackJobs.func1(0xc005573ef0?, 0x0)
        /build/vendor/github.com/operator-framework/operator-lifecycle-manager/pkg/controller/bundle/bundle_unpacker.go:889 +0x1f0
sort.insertionSort_func({0xc0059b3090?, 0xc004d39a80?}, 0x0, 0x5)

```java
4.18.24
```

```java
Inconsistent, appears to require some variation of the Jobs failing
